arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا جَرِيرٌ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، فِي قَوْلِهِ تَعَالَى: " {وَمَنْ قَتَلَ مُؤْمِنًا خَطَأً فَتَحْرِيرُ رَقَبَةٍ مُؤْمِنَةٍ وَدِيَةٌ مُسَلَّمَةٌ إِلَى أَهْلِهِ} إِذَا قُتِلَ الْمُسْلِمُ فَهَذَا لَهُ وَلِوَرَثَتِهِ الْمُسْلِمِينَ {فَإِنْ كَانَ مِنْ قَوْمٍ عَدُوٍّ لَكُمْ وَهُوَ مُؤْمِنٌ} [النساء: 92] الرَّجُلُ يُقْتَلُ وَقَوْمُهُ مُشْرِكُونَ , لَيْسَ بَيْنَهُمْ وَبَيْنَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ عَهْدٌ - - فَعَلَيْهِ {تَحْرِيرُ رَقَبَةٍ مُؤْمِنَةٍ} [النساء: 92]، وَإِنْ قُتِلَ مُسْلِمٌ مِنْ قَوْمٍ مُشْرِكِينَ بَيْنَهُمْ وَبَيْنَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ عَهْدٌ، فَيَكُونُ مِيرَاثُهُ لِلْمُسْلِمِينَ , وَيَكُونُ عَقْلُهُ لِلْمُشْرِكِينَ الَّذِينَ بَيْنَهُمْ وَبَيْنَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ عَهْدٌ , فَيَرِثُ الْمُسْلِمُونَ مِيرَاثَهُ , وَيَكُونُ عَقْلُهُ لِقَوْمِهِ لِأَنَّهُمْ يَعْقِلُونَ عَنْهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Jarir -> Mughirah -> Ibrahim
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Jarīr narrated to us, from Mughīrah, from Ibrāhīm, regarding the saying of the Exalted: "{And whoever kills a believer by mistake - then the freeing of a believing slave and a compensation payment presented to the deceased's family}" [An-Nisa: 92]. If a Muslim is killed, this belongs to him and his Muslim heirs. "{But if the deceased was from a people at war with you and he was a believer}" [An-Nisa: 92] - A man is killed while his people are polytheists, with no covenant between them and the Messenger of Allah ﷺ - upon him is "{the freeing of a believing slave}" [An-Nisa: 92]. And if a Muslim is killed from a polytheist people who have a covenant between them and the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, his inheritance belongs to the Muslims, and his blood money belongs to the polytheists who have a covenant between them and the Messenger of Allah ﷺ. So the Muslims inherit his inheritance, and his blood money belongs to his people because they pay blood money on his behalf.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ إِدْرِيسَ، عَنْ عِيسَى بْنِ مُغِيرَةَ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ، فِي قَوْلِهِ: {وَإِنْ كَانَ مِنْ قَوْمٍ بَيْنَكُمْ وَبَيْنَهُمْ مِيثَاقٌ} [النساء: 92]، قَالَ: مِنْ أَهْلِ الْعَهْدِ وَلَيْسَ بِمُؤْمِنٍ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Ibn Idris -> Isa b. Mughirah -> Al-Sha'bi
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ibn Idrīs narrated to us, from ‘Īsā ibn Mughīrah, from Al-Sha‘bī, regarding His saying: "{And if he was from a people between you and whom is a treaty}" [An-Nisa: 92], he said: "From the people of the covenant, and he is not a believer."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مُعَاوِيَةَ بْنُ هِشَامٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَمَّارُ بْنُ رُزَيْقٍ، عَنْ عَطَاءِ بْنِ السَّائِبِ، عَنْ أَبِي يَحْيَى، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ: {فَإِنْ كَانَ مِنْ قَوْمٍ عَدُوٍّ لَكُمْ وَهُوَ مُؤْمِنٌ فَتَحْرِيرُ رَقَبَةٍ مُؤْمِنَةٍ} [النساء: 92]، قَالَ: كَانَ الرَّجُلُ يَأْتِي النَّبِيَّ عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ فَيُسْلِمُ , ثُمَّ يَرْجِعُ إِلَى قَوْمِهِ فَيَكُونُ فِيهِمْ وَهُمْ مُشْرِكُونَ , فَيُصِيبُهُ الْمُسْلِمُونَ خَطَأً فِي سَرِيَّةٍ أَوْ غَزَاةٍ , فَيُعْتِقُ الَّذِي يُصِيبُهُ رَقَبَةً {وَإِنْ كَانَ مِنْ قَوْمٍ بَيْنَكُمْ وَبَيْنَهُمْ مِيثَاقٌ} [النساء: 92]، قَالَ: هُوَ الرَّجُلُ يَكُونُ مُعَاهَدًا وَيَكُونُ قَوْمُهُ أَهْلَ عَهْدٍ، فَيُسْلَمُ إِلَيْهِمْ الدِّيَةُ وَيُعْتِقُ الَّذِي أَصَابَهُ رَقَبَةً

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Mu'awiyah b. Hisham -> Ammar b. Ruzayq -> 'Ata' b. al-Sa'ib -> Abu Yahya -> Ibn Abbas
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Mu‘āwiyah ibn Hishām narrated to us, saying: ‘Ammār ibn Ruzayq narrated to us, from ‘Aṭā’ ibn al-Sā’ib, from Abū Yaḥyā, from Ibn ‘Abbās: "{But if the deceased was from a people at war with you and he was a believer - then [only] the freeing of a believing slave}" [An-Nisa: 92]. He said: A man would come to the Prophet (peace be upon him) and accept Islam, then return to his people and be among them while they were polytheists. Then the Muslims would kill him by mistake in a raiding party or expedition. So the one who killed him would free a slave. "{And if he was from a people between you and whom is a treaty}" [An-Nisa: 92], he said: "He is a man who has a covenant, and his people are people of a covenant. So the blood money is delivered to them, and the one who killed him frees a slave."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا يَحْيَى بْنُ عَبْدِ الْمَلِكِ بْنِ أَبِي غَنِيَّةَ، عَنِ الْحَكَمِ: أَنَّ الْعَبَّاسَ بْنَ عَبْدِ الْمُطَّلِبِ، لَطَمَ رَجُلًا فَأَقَادَهُ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ مِنَ الْعَبَّاسِ , فَعَفَا عَنْهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Yahya b. Abd al-Malik b. Abi Ghaniyyah -> Al-Hakam
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Yaḥyā ibn ‘Abd al-Malik ibn Abī Ghaniyyah narrated to us, from Al-Ḥakam: That Al-‘Abbās ibn ‘Abd al-Muṭṭalib slapped a man, so the Prophet ﷺ ordered retaliation against Al-‘Abbās, but the man pardoned him.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ الْمَسْعُودِيُّ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَبْدِ الْمَلِكِ بْنِ أَبِي عُبَيْدَةَ، عَنْ نَاجِيَةَ أَبِي الْحَسَنِ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ: أَنَّ عَلِيًّا أُتِيَ فِي رَجُلٍ لَطَمَ رَجُلًا، فَقَالَ لِلْمَلْطُومِ: اقْتَصَّ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Abu Abd al-Rahman al-Mas'udi -> Abd Allah b. Abd al-Malik b. Abi Ubaydah -> Najiyah Abu al-Hasan -> His Father
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Abū ‘Abd al-Raḥmān al-Mas‘ūdī narrated to us, from ‘Abd Allāh ibn ‘Abd al-Malik ibn Abī ‘Ubaydah, from Nājiyah Abū al-Ḥasan, from his father: That ‘Alī was brought regarding a man who slapped another man. He said to the slapped one: "Take retaliation."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ صَالِحٍ، عَنْ مُخَارِقٍ، عَنْ طَارِقِ بْنِ شِهَابِ: أَنَّ خَالِدَ بْنَ الْوَلِيدِ أَقَادَ رَجُلًا مِنْ مُرَادٍ مِنْ لَطْمَةٍ لَطَمَ ابْنَ أَخِيهِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Waki' -> Al-Hasan b. Salih -> Mukhariq -> Tariq b. Shihab
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Wakī‘ narrated to us, from Al-Ḥasan ibn Ṣāliḥ, from Mukhāriq, from Ṭāriq ibn Shihāb: "That Khālid ibn al-Walīd allowed a man from Murād to take retaliation for a slap he gave to his nephew."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا شَرِيكٍ، عَنْ أَبِي إِسْحَاقَ، عَنْ شُرَيْحٍ: أَنَّهُ أَقَادَ مِنْ لَطْمَةٍ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Sharik -> Abu Ishaq -> Shurayh
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Sharīk narrated to us, from Abū Isḥāq, from Shurayḥ: "That he ordered retaliation for a slap."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ عُيَيْنَةَ، عَنْ عَمْرٍو، عَنِ ابْنِ الزُّبَيْرِ: أَنَّهُ أَقَادَ مِنْ لَطْمَةٍ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Ibn Uyaynah -> Amr -> Ibn al-Zubayr
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ibn ‘Uyaynah narrated to us, from ‘Amr, from Ibn al-Zubayr: "That he ordered retaliation for a slap."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ الْمَسْعُودِيُّ، عَنْ زُرَارَةَ بْنِ يَحْيَى، عَنْ أَبِيهِ: أَنَّ الْمُغِيرَةَ بْنَ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ أَقَادَ مِنْ لَطْمَةٍ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Abu Abd al-Rahman al-Mas'udi -> Zurarah b. Yahya -> His Father
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Abū ‘Abd al-Raḥmān al-Mas‘ūdī narrated to us, from Zurārah ibn Yaḥyā, from his father: "That Al-Mughīrah ibn ‘Abd Allāh ordered retaliation for a slap."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا شَبَابَةُ، عَنْ يَحْيَى بْنِ الْحُصَيْنِ، قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ طَارِقَ بْنَ شِهَابٍ، يَقُولُ: لَطَمَ أَبُو بَكْرٍ يَوْمًا رَجُلًا لَطْمَةً , فَقِيلَ: مَا رَأَيْنَا كَالْيَوْمِ قَطُّ هَنْعَةً وَلَطْمَةً , فَقَالَ أَبُو بَكْرٍ: "إِنَّ هَذَا أَتَانِي يَسْتَحْمِلُنِي , فَحَمَلْتُهُ , فَإِذَا هُوَ يَتْبَعُهُمْ فَحَلَفْتُ أَنْ لَا أَحْمِلَهُ: وَاللَّهِ لَا أَحْمِلُهُ ثَلَاثَ مَرَّاتٍ , ثُمَّ قَالَ لَهُ: اقْتَصَّ" فَعَفَا الرَّجُلُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Shababah -> Yahya b. al-Husayn -> Tariq b. Shihab
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Shabābah narrated to us, from Yaḥyā ibn al-Ḥuṣayn, who said: I heard Ṭāriq ibn Shihāb saying: Abu Bakr one day slapped a man. It was said: "We have never seen anything like today, a mark (from illness) and a slap." Abu Bakr said: "This man came to me asking for a mount, so I gave him a mount. Then I saw him following them, so I swore not to give him a mount: 'By Allah, I will not give him a mount,' three times. Then he said to him: 'Take retaliation.'" But the man pardoned him.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ حَسَنِ بْنِ صَالِحٍ، قَالَ: قُلْتُ لِابْنِ أَبِي لَيْلَى: أَقَدْتَ مِنْ لَطْمَةٍ , قَالَ: نَعَمْ وَمِنْ لَطَمَاتٍ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Waki' -> Hasan b. Salih -> Ibn Abi Layla
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Wakī‘ narrated to us, from Ḥasan ibn Ṣāliḥ, who said: I said to Ibn Abī Laylā: "Did you order retaliation for a slap?" He said: "Yes, and for slaps."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا يَحْيَى بْنُ يَمَانٍ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ جَابِرٍ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ، عَنْ مَسْرُوقٍ: أَنَّهُ أَقَادَ مِنْ لَطْمَةٍ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Yahya b. Yaman -> Sufyan -> Jabir -> Al-Sha'bi -> Masruq
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Yaḥyā ibn Yamān narrated to us, from Sufyān, from Jābir, from Al-Sha‘bī, from Masrūq: "That he ordered retaliation for a slap."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ: أَنَّ جِلْوَازًا قَنَعَ رَجُلًا بِالسَّوْطِ , فَأَقَادَهُ مِنْهُ شُرَيْحٌ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Waki' -> Sufyan -> Mughirah -> Ibrahim
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Wakī‘ narrated to us, saying: Sufyān narrated to us, from Mughīrah, from Ibrāhīm: "That a policeman struck a man on the head with a whip, so Shurayḥ ordered retaliation from him."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو خَالِدٍ، عَنْ أَشْعَثَ، عَنْ فُضَيْلٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ مَعْقِلٍ، قَالَ: كُنْتُ جَالِسًا عِنْدَ عَلِيٍّ، فَجَاءَهُ رَجُلٌ فَسَارَّهُ فَقَالَ عَلِيٌّ: يَا قَنْبَرُ، فَقَالَ النَّاسُ: يَا قَنْبَرُ , قَالَ: أَخْرِجْ هَذَا فَاجْلِدْهُ , ثُمَّ جَاءَ الْمَجْلُودُ، فَقَالَ: إِنَّهُ قَدْ زَادَ عَلَيَّ ثَلَاثَةَ أَسْوَاطٍ , فَقَالَ عَلِيٌّ: مَا تَقُولُ؟ قَالَ: صَدَقَ يَا أَمِيرَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ , قَالَ: خُذِ السَّوْطَ فَاجْلِدْهُ ثَلَاثَةَ أَسْوَاطٍ , ثُمَّ قَالَ: يَا قَنْبَرُ , إِذَا جَلَدْتَ فَلَا تَعْدُ الْحُدُودَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Abu Khalid -> Ash'ath -> Fudayl -> Abd Allah b. Ma'qil
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Abū Khālid narrated to us, from Ash‘ath, from Fuḍayl, from ‘Abd Allāh ibn Ma‘qil, who said: I was sitting with ‘Alī, and a man came to him and whispered something to him. ‘Alī said: "O Qanbar!" The people said: "O Qanbar!" He said: "Take this man out and flog him." Then the flogged man came and said: "He added three lashes to me." ‘Alī said: "What do you say?" He said: "He spoke the truth, O Commander of the Faithful." He said: "Take the whip and flog him three lashes." Then he said: "O Qanbar, when you flog, do not exceed the limits."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا حَفْصٌ، عَنِ الشَّيْبَانِيِّ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ، وَالْحَكَمِ، وَحَمَّادٍ، قَالُوا: مَا أُصِيبَ بِهِ مِنْ سَوْطٍ أَوْ عَصًا أَوْ حَجَرٍ فَكَانَ دُونَ النَّفْسِ فَهُوَ عَمْدٌ , دِيَتُهُ الْقَوَدُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Hafs -> Al-Shaybani -> Al-Sha'bi / Al-Hakam / Hammad
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ḥafṣ narrated to us, from Al-Shaybānī, from Al-Sha‘bī, Al-Ḥakam, and Ḥammād, who said: "Whatever injury is caused by a whip, a stick, or a stone, and is less than killing, it is intentional; its compensation is retaliation."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا جَرِيرٌ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، فِي رَجُلٍ اسْتَعَارَ مِنْ رَجُلٍ فَرَسًا فَرَكَّضَهُ حَتَّى مَاتَ , قَالَ: لَيْسَ عَلَيْهِ ضَمَانٌ لِأَنَّ الرَّجُلَ يُرْكِضُ فَرَسَهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Jarir -> Mughirah -> Ibrahim
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Jarīr narrated to us, from Mughīrah, from Ibrāhīm, regarding a man who borrowed a horse from another man and galloped it until it died. He said: "He is not liable, because a man gallops his horse."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ إِسْرَائِيلَ، عَنْ جَابِرٍ، عَنْ عَامِرٍ، فِي رَجُلٍ أَعْطَى رَجُلًا فَرَسًا فَقَتَلَهُ , قَالَ: لَا يَضْمَنُ إِلَّا أَنْ يَكُونَ عَبْدًا أَوْ صَبِيًّا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Waki' -> Isra'il -> Jabir -> 'Amir
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Wakī‘ narrated to us, from Isrā’īl, from Jābir, from ‘Āmir, regarding a man who gave another man a horse and he killed it. He said: "He is not liable unless he is a slave or a child."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا يَحْيَى بْنُ آدَمَ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا زُهَيْرٌ، عَنْ جَابِرٍ، عَنْ عَامِرٍ، فِي رَجُلٍ قَتَلَ رَجُلًا قَدْ ذَهَبَتِ الرُّوحُ مِنْ نِصْفِ جَسَدِهِ , قَالَ: يَضْمَنُهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Yahya b. Adam -> Zuhayr -> Jabir -> 'Amir
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Yaḥyā ibn Ādam narrated to us, saying: Zuhayr narrated to us, from Jābir, from ‘Āmir, regarding a man who killed a man whose soul had departed from half his body (paralyzed). He said: "He is liable for him."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ فُضَيْلٍ، عَنْ أَشْعَثَ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ، قَالَ: مَنْ أَوْقَفَ دَابَّتَهُ فِي طَرِيقِ الْمُسْلِمِينَ أَوْ وَضَعَ شَيْئًا، فَهُوَ ضَامِنٌ لِجِنَايَتِهِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Ibn Fudayl -> Ash'ath -> Al-Sha'bi
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ibn Fuḍayl narrated to us, from Ash‘ath, from Al-Sha‘bī, who said: "Whoever stops his beast in the Muslims' road or places something there, he is liable for its crime (damage caused)."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ نُمَيْرٍ، عَنْ أَشْعَثَ، وَعَنْ حَمَّادٍ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: مَنْ رَبَطَ دَابَّةً فِي طَرِيقٍ فَهُوَ ضَامِنٌ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Abd Allah b. Numayr -> Ash'ath / Hammad -> Ibrahim
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: ‘Abd Allāh ibn Numayr narrated to us, from Ash‘ath, and from Ḥammād, from Ibrāhīm, who said: "Whoever ties a beast in a road is liable."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ أَبِي عَدِيٍّ، عَنْ أَشْعَثَ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ: أَنَّهُ كَانَ لَا يُوَقِّتُ فِي الْهَاشِمَةِ شَيْئًا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Muhammad b. Abi Adi -> Ash'ath -> Al-Hasan
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Muḥammad ibn Abī ‘Adī narrated to us, from Ash‘ath, from Al-Ḥasan: "That he did not set a specific time limit for the Hāshimah (a wound that crushes bone)."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا زَيْدُ بْنُ الْحُبَابِ، عَنْ حَمَّادِ بْنِ سَلَمَةَ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ: أَنَّ عَبْدَ الْمَلِكِ بْنَ مَرْوَانَ، قَضَى فِي الدَّامِيَةِ بِبَعِيرٍ , وَفِي الْبَاضِعَةِ بِبَعِيرَيْنِ , وَقَضَى فِي الْمُتَلَاحِمَةِ بِثَلَاثَةِ أَبْعِرَةٍ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Zayd b. al-Hubab -> Hammad b. Salamah -> Qatadah
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Zayd ibn al-Ḥubāb narrated to us, from Ḥammād ibn Salamah, from Qatādah: "That ‘Abd al-Malik ibn Marwān judged for the Dāmiyah (wound that bleeds) one camel, for the Bāḍi‘ah (wound that cuts skin) two camels, and for the Mutalāḥimah (wound that cuts into flesh) three camels."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا جَرِيرٌ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، وَعَنْ حَمَّادٍ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، فِي الْعَبْدَيْنِ يَفْقَأُ أَحَدُهُمَا عَيْنَ صَاحِبِهِ , قَالَ: إِنْ كَانَتْ قِيمَتُهُمَا سَوَاءً فَالْعَيْنُ بِالْعَيْنِ , وَإِنْ كَانَتْ قِيمَةُ أَحَدِهِمَا أَكْثَرَ مِنَ الْآخَرِ رَدَّ الْأَكْثَرُ عَلَى الْأَقَلِّ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Jarir -> Mughirah / Hammad -> Ibrahim
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Jarīr narrated to us, from Mughīrah, from Ibrāhīm, and from Ḥammād, from Ibrāhīm, regarding two slaves, one of whom gouges out the eye of his companion. He said: "If their value is equal, then eye for eye. And if the value of one of them is more than the other, the one with more value returns [the difference] to the one with less."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ بَكْرٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ جُرَيْجٍ، قَالَ: قُلْتُ لِعَطَاءٍ: الْعَبْدُ يَقْتُلُ الْعَبْدَ عَمْدًا , الْمَقْتُولُ خَيْرٌ مِنَ الْقَاتِلِ؟ قَالَ: لَيْسَ لِسَادَةِ الْمَقْتُولِ إِلَّا قَاتِلُ عَبْدِهِمْ , إِنْ شَاءُوا قَتَلُوهُ , وَإِنْ شَاءُوا اسْتَرَقُّوهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Muhammad b. Bakr -> Ibn Jurayj -> 'Ata'
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Muḥammad ibn Bakr narrated to us, from Ibn Jurayj, who said: I said to ‘Aṭā’: "A slave kills another slave intentionally, and the killed one is better than the killer?" He said: "The masters of the killed slave are entitled to nothing but the killer of their slave. If they wish, they can kill him, and if they wish, they can enslave him."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ مُبَارَكٍ، عَنْ مَعْمَرٍ: أَنَّ رَجُلًا مِنْ أَهْلِ الْهِنْدِ قَدِمَ بِأَمَانٍ , فَقَتَلَهُ رَجُلٌ مِنَ الْمُسْلِمِينَ بِأَخِيهِ , فَكَتَبَ فِي ذَلِكَ إِلَى عُمَرَ بْنِ عَبْدِ الْعَزِيزِ فَكَتَبَ أَنْ: لَا تَقْتُلْهُ وَخُذْ مِنْهُ الدِّيَةَ، فَابْعَثْ بِهَا إِلَى وَرَثَتِهِ , وَأَمَرَ بِهِ فَسُجِنَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Ibn Mubarak -> Ma'mar
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ibn Mubārak narrated to us, from Ma‘mar: That a man from the people of India came with a pledge of security, and a Muslim man killed him in retaliation for his brother. This was written to ‘Umar ibn ‘Abd al-‘Azīz, and he wrote back: "Do not kill him, but take the blood money from him and send it to his heirs." And he ordered him to be imprisoned.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ مَهْدِيٍّ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ يُوسُفَ بْنِ يَعْقُوبَ: أَنَّ رَجُلًا مِنَ الْمُشْرِكِينَ قَتَلَ رَجُلًا مِنَ الْمُسْلِمِينَ، ثُمَّ دَخَلَ بِأَمَانٍ فَقَتَلَهُ أَخُوهُ , فَقَضَى عَلَيْهِ عُمَرُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الْعَزِيزِ بِالدِّيَةِ وَجَعَلَهُ عَلَيْهِ فِي مَالِهِ , وَحَبَسَهُ فِي السِّجْنِ , وَبَعَثَ بِدِيَتِهِ إِلَى وَرَثَتِهِ مِنْ أَهْلِ الْحَرْبِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Ibn Mahdi -> Sufyan -> Yusuf b. Ya'qub
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ibn Mahdī narrated to us, from Sufyān, from Yūsuf ibn Ya‘qūb: "That a man from the polytheists killed a Muslim man, then entered with a pledge of security, and his brother killed him. So ‘Umar ibn ‘Abd al-‘Azīz judged against him for the blood money and made it payable from his own wealth, imprisoned him, and sent his blood money to his heirs among the people of war."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا الثَّقَفِيُّ، عَنْ حَبِيبٍ الْمُعَلِّمِ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ: أَنَّ رَجُلًا مِنَ الْمُشْرِكِينَ حَجَّ , فَلَمَّا رَجَعَ صَادِرًا لَقِيَهُ رَجُلٌ مِنَ الْمُسْلِمِينَ فَقَتَلَهُ , فَأَمَرَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ أَنْ تُؤَدَّى دِيَتُهُ إِلَى أَهْلِهِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Al-Thaqafi -> Habib al-Mu'allim -> Al-Hasan
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Al-Thaqafī narrated to us, from Ḥabīb al-Mu‘allim, from Al-Ḥasan: "That a man from the polytheists performed Hajj. When he returned, leaving, a Muslim man met him and killed him. So the Prophet ﷺ ordered that his blood money be paid to his family."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو دَاوُدَ، عَنْ أَبِي حُرَّةَ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، فِي قَوْمٍ لَقُوا عَدُوًّا، فَاسْتَأْجَلُوهُمْ خَمْسَةَ أَيَّامٍ فَقُتِلَ بَيْنَهُمْ قَتِيلٌ , قَالَ: عَلَى الْمُسْلِمِينَ دِيَتُهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Abu Dawud -> Abu Hurrah -> Al-Hasan
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Abū Dāwūd narrated to us, from Abū Ḥurrah, from Al-Ḥasan, regarding a people who met an enemy and asked for a respite of five days, and a person was killed among them. He said: "His blood money is upon the Muslims."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا حَفْصٌ، عَنْ أَبِي طَلْقٍ، عَنْ أُخْتِهِ هِنْدَ بِنْتِ طَلْقٍ، قَالَتْ: كُنْتُ فِي نِسْوَةٍ وَصَبِيٌّ مُسَجَّى , قَالَتْ: فَمَرَّتِ امْرَأَةٌ فَوَطَأَتُهُ , قَالَتْ أُمُّ الصَّبِيِّ: قَتَلَتْهُ وَاللَّهِ، قَالَتْ: فَشَهِدْنَ عِنْدَ عَلِيٍّ عَشْرُ نِسْوَةٍ وَأَنَا عَاشِرَتُهُنَّ: فَقَضَى عَلَيْهَا بِالدِّيَةِ وَأَعَانَهَا بِأَلْفَيْنِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Hafs -> Abu Talq -> His Sister Hind bint Talq
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ḥafṣ narrated to us, from Abū Ṭalq, from his sister Hind bint Ṭalq, who said: I was among some women, and a boy was covered up. She said: A woman passed by and stepped on him. The boy's mother said: "She killed him, by Allah!" She said: So ten women testified before ‘Alī, and I was the tenth of them. He judged against her for the blood money and helped her with two thousand.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ مُبَارَكٍ، عَنْ أَبِي حَنِيفَةَ، عَنْ حَمَّادٍ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: لَا يَكُونُ التَّغْلِيظُ فِي شَيْءٍ مِنَ الدِّيَةِ إِلَّا فِي الْإِبِلِ , وَالتَّغْلِيظُ فِي إِنَاثِ الْإِبِلِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Ibn Mubarak -> Abu Hanifah -> Hammad -> Ibrahim
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ibn Mubārak narrated to us, from Abū Ḥanīfah, from Ḥammād, from Ibrāhīm, who said: "There is no aggravation in any part of the blood money except in camels, and the aggravation is in the female camels."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مَعْنُ بْنُ عِيسَى، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي ذِئْبٍ، عَنِ الزُّهْرِيِّ، فِي امْرَأَةٍ ضُرِبَتْ، فَأَسْقَطَتْ ثَلَاثَةَ أَسْقَاطٍ , قَالَ: أَرَى أَنَّ فِي كُلِّ وَاحِدٍ مِنْهُمْ غُرَّةً كَمَا أَنَّ فِي كُلِّ وَاحِدٍ مِنْهُمُ الدِّيَةَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Ma'n b. Isa -> Ibn Abi Dhi'b -> Al-Zuhri
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ma‘n ibn ‘Īsā narrated to us, from Ibn Abī Dhi’b, from Al-Zuhrī, regarding a woman who was struck and miscarried three fetuses. He said: "I see that for each one of them there is a Ghurrah (a slave or its value), just as for each one of them there is blood money."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مَعْنُ بْنُ عِيسَى، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي ذِئْبٍ، عَنِ الزُّهْرِيِّ، قَالَ: أَرَى الْعُطَاسَ اسْتِهْلَالًا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Ma'n b. Isa -> Ibn Abi Dhi'b -> Al-Zuhri
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ma‘n ibn ‘Īsā narrated to us, from Ibn Abī Dhi’b, from Al-Zuhrī, who said: "I consider sneezing to be Istihlāl (signs of life at birth)."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا إِسْرَائِيلُ، عَنْ سِمَاكٍ، عَنْ عِكْرِمَةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، قَالَ: اسْتِهْلَالُهُ صِيَاحُهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Waki' -> Isra'il -> Simak -> Ikrimah -> Ibn Abbas
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Wakī‘ narrated to us, saying: Isrā’īl narrated to us, from Simāk, from ‘Ikrimah, from Ibn ‘Abbās, who said: "His Istihlāl is his crying."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ مَهْدِيٍّ، عَنْ سُلَيْمَانَ بْنِ بِلَالٍ، عَنْ يَحْيَى بْنِ سَعِيدٍ، عَنِ الْقَاسِمِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ، قَالَ: "الِاسْتِهْلَالُ: النِّدَاءُ أَوِ الْعُطَاسُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Ibn Mahdi -> Sulayman b. Bilal -> Yahya b. Sa'id -> Al-Qasim b. Muhammad
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ibn Mahdī narrated to us, from Sulaymān ibn Bilāl, from Yaḥyā ibn Sa‘īd, from Al-Qāsim ibn Muḥammad, who said: "Istihlāl is calling out or sneezing."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ مَهْدِيٍّ، عَنْ زَائِدَةَ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: الِاسْتِهْلَالُ الصِّيَاحُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Ibn Mahdi -> Za'idah -> Mughirah -> Ibrahim
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ibn Mahdī narrated to us, from Zā’idah, from Mughīrah, from Ibrāhīm, who said: "Istihlāl is crying."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عِيسَى بْنُ يُونُسَ، عَنْ صَاعِدِ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ: فِي اللِّحْيَةِ الدِّيَةُ إِذَا نُتِفَتْ، فَلَمْ تَنْبُتْ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Isa b. Yunus -> Sa'id b. Muslim -> Al-Sha'bi
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: ‘Īsā ibn Yūnus narrated to us, from Ṣā‘id ibn Muslim, from Al-Sha‘bī: "There is blood money for the beard if it is plucked and does not grow back."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا غُنْدَرٌ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، عَنْ عَاصِمٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ سِيرِينَ، قَالَ: كَانَ عُمَرُ بْنُ الْخَطَّابِ يُعَدِّي الْمَمْلُوكَ عَلَى سَيِّدِهِ إِذَا اسْتَعْدَاهُ "قَالَ مُحَمَّدٌ: اسْتَعْدَى أَبِي عَلَى ابْنِ عُمَرَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Ghundar -> Shu'bah -> Asim -> Ibn Sirin
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ghundar narrated to us, from Shu‘bah, from ‘Āṣim, from Ibn Sīrīn, who said: ‘Umar ibn al-Khaṭṭāb used to grant retaliation to a slave against his master if he sought it. Muḥammad said: My father sought retaliation against Ibn ‘Umar.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ إِدْرِيسَ، عَنْ مُطَرِّفٍ، عَنِ الْحَارِثِ: أَنَّ عَبْدًا أَتَى عَلِيًّا قَدْ وَسَمَهُ أَهْلُهُ، فَأَعْتَقَهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Ibn Idris -> Mutarrif -> Al-Harith
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ibn Idrīs narrated to us, from Muṭarrif, from Al-Ḥārith: "That a slave came to ‘Alī whom his owners had branded, so he freed him."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا جَرِيرٌ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، عَنْ حَمَّادٍ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: إِذَا دَخَلَ اللِّصُّ دَارَ الرَّجُلِ فَقَتَلَهُ، فَلَا ضِرَارَ عَلَيْهِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Jarir -> Mughirah -> Hammad -> Ibrahim
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Jarīr narrated to us, from Mughīrah, from Ḥammād, from Ibrāhīm, who said: "If a thief enters a man's house and he kills him, there is no harm upon him."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا شَرِيكٌ، عَنْ جَابِرٍ، عَنْ عَامِرٍ، قَالَ: اقْتُلِ اللِّصَّ وَأَنَا ضَامِنٌ، أَلَّا تَتْبَعَكَ تَبِعَةٌ مِنْهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Sharik -> Jabir -> 'Amir
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Sharīk narrated to us, from Jābir, from ‘Āmir, who said: "Kill the thief, and I guarantee that no consequence will follow you from it."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدَةُ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ إِسْحَاقَ، عَنِ الزُّهْرِيِّ، عَنْ سَالِمِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ: أَنَّهُ وَجَدَ سَارِقًا فِي بَيْتِهِ , فَأَصْلَتَ عَلَيْهِ بِالسَّيْفِ , وَلَوْ تَرَكْنَاهُ لَقَتَلَهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> 'Abdah -> Muhammad b. Ishaq -> Al-Zuhri -> Salim b. Abd Allah -> Ibn Umar
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: ‘Abdah narrated to us, from Muḥammad ibn Isḥāq, from Al-Zuhrī, from Sālim ibn ‘Abd Allāh, from Ibn ‘Umar: "That he found a thief in his house, so he drew his sword against him, and if we had left him, he would have killed him."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ عُلَيَّةَ، عَنْ أَيُّوبَ، عَنْ حُمَيْدِ بْنِ هِلَالٍ، عَنْ حُجَيْرِ بْنِ الرَّبِيعِ، قَالَ: قُلْتُ لِعِمْرَانَ بْنِ حُصَيْنٍ: أَرَأَيْتَ إِنْ دَخَلَ عَلَيَّ دَاخِلٌ يُرِيدُ نَفْسِي وَمَالِي , فَقَالَ: لَوْ دَخَلَ عَلَيَّ دَاخِلٌ يُرِيدُ نَفْسِي وَمَالِي لَرَأَيْتُ أَنْ قَدْ حَلَّ لِي قَتْلُهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Ibn Ulayyah -> Ayyub -> Humayd b. Hilal -> Hujayr b. al-Rabi'
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ibn ‘Ulayyah narrated to us, from Ayyūb, from Ḥumayd ibn Hilāl, from Ḥujayr ibn al-Rabī‘, who said: I said to ‘Imrān ibn Ḥuṣayn: "What do you think if someone enters upon me wanting my life and my wealth?" He said: "If someone entered upon me wanting my life and my wealth, I would consider it lawful for me to kill him."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو الْأَحْوَصِ، عَنْ سِمَاكٍ، عَنْ قَابُوسَ بْنِ الْمُخَارِقِ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، قَالَ: أَتَى النَّبِيَّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ رَجُلٌ، فَقَالَ: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ , الرَّجُلُ يَأْتِينِي يُرِيدُ مَالِي , قَالَ: ذَكِّرْهُ بِاللَّهِ , قَالَ: فَإِنْ لَمْ يَذْكُرْ , قَالَ: فَاسْتَعِنْ عَلَيْهِ بِمَنْ حَوْلَكَ مِنَ الْمُسْلِمِينَ , قَالَ: فَإِنْ لَمْ يَكُنْ حَوْلِي أَحَدٌ مِنَ الْمُسْلِمِينَ , قَالَ: فَاسْتَعِنْ عَلَيْهِ بِالسُّلْطَانِ , قَالَ: فَإِنْ نَأَى عَنِّي السُّلْطَانُ؟ قَالَ: فَقَاتِلْ دُونَ مَالِكَ حَتَّى تَمْنَعَ مَالَكَ أَوْ تَكُونَ فِي شُهَدَاءِ الْآخِرَةِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Abu al-Ahwas -> Simak -> Qabus b. al-Mukhariq -> His Father
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Abū al-Aḥwaṣ narrated to us, from Simāk, from Qābūs ibn al-Mukhāriq, from his father, who said: A man came to the Prophet ﷺ and said: "O Messenger of Allah, a man comes to me wanting my wealth." He said: "Remind him of Allah." He said: "What if he does not remember?" He said: "Seek help against him from the Muslims around you." He said: "What if there is no Muslim around me?" He said: "Seek help against him from the ruler." He said: "What if the ruler is far from me?" He said: "Then fight for your wealth until you protect your wealth or become one of the martyrs of the Hereafter."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ إِدْرِيسَ، عَنْ هِشَامٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ سِيرِينَ، قَالَ: سَمِعْتُهُ يَقُولُ: مَا عَلِمْتُ أَنَّ أَحَدًا مِنَ الْمُسْلِمِينَ تَرَكَ قِتَالَ رَجُلٍ يَقْطَعُ عَلَيْهِ الطَّرِيقَ أَوْ يَطْرُقُهُ فِي بَيْتِهِ تَأَثُّمًا مِنْ ذَلِكَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Ibn Idris -> Hisham -> Ibn Sirin
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ibn Idrīs narrated to us, from Hishām, from Ibn Sīrīn, who said: I heard him say: "I do not know of any Muslim who refrained from fighting a man who waylays him or attacks him in his house out of fear of sinning."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبَّادُ بْنُ عَوْفٍ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، قَالَ: اقْتُلِ اللِّصَّ وَالْحَرُورِيَّ وَالْمُسْتَعْرِضَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Abbad b. Awf -> Al-Hasan
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: ‘Abbād ibn ‘Awf narrated to us, from Al-Ḥasan, who said: "Kill the thief, the Haruri (Kharijite), and the one who attacks indiscriminately."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ إِدْرِيسَ، عَنْ عُبَيْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عُمَرَ، عَنْ نَافِعٍ، قَالَ: أَصْلَتَ ابْنُ عُمَرَ عَلَى لِصٍّ بِالسَّيْفِ , فَلَوْ تَرَكْنَاهُ لَقَتَلَهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Ibn Idris -> Ubayd Allah b. Umar -> Nafi'
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ibn Idrīs narrated to us, from ‘Ubayd Allāh ibn ‘Umar, from Nāfi‘, who said: "Ibn ‘Umar drew his sword against a thief, and if we had left him, he would have killed him."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ عُيَيْنَةَ، عَنِ الزُّهْرِيِّ، عَنْ طَلْحَةَ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ زَيْدٍ، قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: مَنْ قُتِلَ دُونَ مَالِهِ فَهُوَ شَهِيدٌ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Ibn Uyaynah -> Al-Zuhri -> Talhah b. Abd Allah -> Sa'id b. Zayd
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ibn ‘Uyaynah narrated to us, from Al-Zuhrī, from Ṭalḥah ibn ‘Abd Allāh, from Sa‘īd ibn Zayd, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Whoever is killed protecting his property is a martyr."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ عُيَيْنَةَ، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ دِينَارٍ، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ شُعَيْبٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو، عَنِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ، قَالَ: مَنْ قُتِلَ دُونَ مَالِهِ فَهُوَ شَهِيدٌ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Ibn Uyaynah -> Amr b. Dinar -> Amr b. Shu'ayb -> Abd Allah b. Amr
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ibn ‘Uyaynah narrated to us, from ‘Amr ibn Dīnār, from ‘Amr ibn Shu‘ayb, from ‘Abd Allāh ibn ‘Amr, from the Prophet ﷺ, who said: "Whoever is killed protecting his property is a martyr."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مَرْوَانُ، عَنْ يَزِيدَ بْنِ سِنَانٍ، عَنْ مَيْمُونِ بْنِ مِهْرَانَ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ، قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: مَنْ قُتِلَ دُونَ مَالِهِ فَهُوَ شَهِيدٌ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Marwan -> Yazid b. Sinan -> Maymun b. Mihran -> Ibn Umar
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Marwān narrated to us, from Yazīd ibn Sinān, from Maymūn ibn Mihrān, from Ibn ‘Umar, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Whoever is killed protecting his property is a martyr."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ جَابِرٍ، عَنْ عَامِرٍ، قَالَ: سَأَلَهُ ابْنُ هُبَيْرَةَ عَنِ الْعَقْلِ عَلَى رُءُوسِ الرِّجَالِ أَوْ عَلَى الْأَعْطِيَةِ , قَالَ: لَا , بَلْ عَلَى رُءُوسِ الرِّجَالِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Waki' -> Sufyan -> Jabir -> 'Amir
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Wakī‘ narrated to us, from Sufyān, from Jābir, from ‘Āmir, who said: Ibn Hubayrah asked him whether the blood money (Aqilah) is upon the heads of men or upon the stipends. He said: "No, rather upon the heads of men."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ سُفْيَانَ، يَقُولُ: الْعَقْلُ وَالْقَسَامَةُ وَالشُّفْعَةُ عَلَى رُءُوسِ الرِّجَالِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Waki' -> Sufyan
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Wakī‘ narrated to us, saying: I heard Sufyān say: "Blood money (Aql), oaths (Qasāmah), and preemption (Shuf‘ah) are upon the heads of men."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا جَرِيرٌ عَنْ رَقَبَةَ، عَنْ حَمَّادٍ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، فِي الْجَرَّةِ تُوضَعُ عَلَى الْجِدَارِ فَتُصِيبُ إِنْسَانًا , قَالَ: إِنْ كَانَ أَصْلُ الْجِدَارِ لِصَاحِبِ الْجَرَّةِ لَمْ يَضْمَنْ مَا أَصَابَتْ فِي الشَّيْءِ يُوضَعُ عَلَى الشَّيْءِ مِنْ مِلْكِهِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Jarir -> Raqabah -> Hammad -> Ibrahim
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Jarīr narrated to us, from Raqabah, from Ḥammād, from Ibrāhīm, regarding a jar placed on a wall that hits a person. He said: "If the base of the wall belongs to the owner of the jar, he is not liable for what it hit, regarding something placed on something he owns."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مُعَاذُ بْنُ مُعَاذٍ، قَالَ: أَخْبَرَنِي أَشْعَثُ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ: أَنَّهُ كَانَ لَا يَرَى أَنْ يَقْتَصَّ الرَّجُلُ مِنَ الرَّجُلَيْنِ فِيمَا دُونَ النَّفْسِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Mu'adh b. Mu'adh -> Ash'ath -> Al-Hasan
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Mu‘ādh ibn Mu‘ādh narrated to us, saying: Ash‘ath informed me, from Al-Ḥasan: "That he did not see that a man should take retaliation from two men for anything less than life."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْأَعْلَى، عَنْ مَعْمَرٍ، عَنِ الزُّهْرِيِّ، كَانَ يَقُولُ: إِذَا قُتِلَتِ الْمَرْأَةُ وَهِيَ حَامِلٌ فِدْيَةٌ وَغُرَّةٌ وَإِنْ لَمْ تُلْقِهِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Abd al-A'la -> Ma'mar -> Al-Zuhri
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: ‘Abd al-A‘lā narrated to us, from Ma‘mar, from Al-Zuhrī, who used to say: "If a pregnant woman is killed, there is a ransom and a Ghurrah (indemnity for the fetus), even if she did not expel it."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ سَوَاءٍ، عَنْ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، فِي الْمَرْأَةِ تُقْتَلُ وَهِيَ حَامِلٌ فِي جَنِينِهَا شَيْءٌ؟ قَالَ: "كَانَ يَقُولُ: لَيْسَ فِيهَا شَيْءٌ حَتَّى تَقْذِفَهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Muhammad b. Sawa' -> Sa'id -> Qatadah
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Muḥammad ibn Sawā’ narrated to us, from Sa‘īd, from Qatādah, regarding a pregnant woman being killed, is there anything for her fetus? He said: "He used to say: There is nothing for it unless she expels it."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا حُمَيْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ، عَنْ مُوسَى بْنِ أَبِي الْفُرَاتِ: أَنَّ عُمَرَ بْنَ عَبْدِ الْعَزِيزِ، قَالَ: أَيُّمَا عَبْدٍ قَتَلَ عَبْدًا عَمْدًا فَاقْتُلْهُ بِهِ , وَثَمَنُ الْأَوَّلِ فَأَخْرِجْهُ مِنْ بَيْتِ الْمَالِ فَأَعْطِهِ مَوَالِيَهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Humayd b. Abd al-Rahman -> Musa b. Abi al-Furat -> Umar b. Abd al-Aziz
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ḥumayd ibn ‘Abd al-Raḥmān narrated to us, from Mūsā ibn Abī al-Furāt: That ‘Umar ibn ‘Abd al-‘Azīz said: "Whichever slave kills another slave intentionally, kill him for it, and take the price of the first one from the Bayt al-Mal and give it to his masters."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرِ بْنُ عَيَّاشٍ، عَنْ عَاصِمٍ، قَالَ: كَتَبَ عَدِيُّ بْنُ أَرْطَاةَ قَاضِي الْبَصْرَةِ إِلَى عُمَرَ بْنِ عَبْدِ الْعَزِيزِ أَنِّي وَجَدْتُ قَتِيلًا فِي سُوقِ الْجَزَّارِينَ , قَالَ: أَمَّا الْقَتِيلُ فَدِيَتُهُ مِنْ بَيْتِ الْمَالِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Abu Bakr b. Ayyash -> Asim
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Abū Bakr ibn ‘Ayyāsh narrated to us, from ‘Āṣim, who said: ‘Adī ibn Arṭāh, the judge of Basra, wrote to ‘Umar ibn ‘Abd al-‘Azīz saying: "I found a murdered person in the butchers' market." He said: "As for the murdered person, his blood money is from the Bayt al-Mal."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا شَبَابَةُ بْنُ سَوَّارٍ، قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ الْحَكَمَ وَحَمَّادًا عَنِ الْمُكَارِي يَسُوقُ بِالْمَرْأَةِ فَأَكْثَرُ عِلْمِي أَنَّهُمَا قَالَا: لَيْسَ عَلَيْهِ ضَمَانٌ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Shababah b. Sawwar -> Al-Hakam / Hammad
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Shabābah ibn Sawwār narrated to us, saying: I asked Al-Ḥakam and Ḥammād about a muleteer driving a woman (on a mount). To the best of my knowledge, they both said: "He is not liable."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو أُسَامَةَ، عَنِ الْمُجَالِدِ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنِي عَرِيفٌ لِجُهَيْنَةَ: أَنَّ النَّبِيَّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ أُوتِيَ بِأَسِيرٍ فِي الشِّتَاءِ فَقَالَ لِأُنَاسٍ مِنْ جُهَيْنَةَ: اذْهَبُوا بِهِ فَادْفُوهُ قَالَ: فَكَانَ الدَّفْوُ بِلِسَانِهِمْ عِنْدَهُمُ الْقَتْلَ فَذَهَبُوا بِهِ فَقَتَلُوهُ , فَسَأَلَهُمُ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ بَعْدُ، فَقَالُوا: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ , أَلَمْ تَأْمُرْنَا أَنْ نَقْتُلَهُ , قَالَ: وَكَيْفَ قُلْتُ لَكُمْ؟ قَالَ: قُلْتَ: اذْهَبُوا بِهِ فَادْفُوهُ , قَالَ: فَقَالَ: قَدْ شَرِكْتُكُمْ , إِذًا اعْقِلُوهُ وَأَنَا شَرِيكُكُمْ، قَالَ: فَحَدَّثْتُ هَذَا الْحَدِيثَ عَامِرًا , قَالَ: صَدَقَ , وَعَرَفَ الْحَدِيثَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Abu Usamah -> Al-Mujalid -> A Leader of Juhaynah -> The Prophet ﷺ
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Abū Usāmah narrated to us, from Al-Mujālid, who said: A leader of Juhaynah narrated to me: That a prisoner was brought to the Prophet ﷺ in winter. He said to some people from Juhaynah: "Take him and keep him warm (adfu'uhu)." He said: "Dafw" in their dialect meant killing. So they took him and killed him. The Prophet ﷺ asked them later, and they said: "O Messenger of Allah, did you not order us to kill him?" He said: "And how did I say it to you?" They said: "You said: 'Take him and keep him warm (adfu'uhu).'" He said: "I have shared with you; so pay the blood money, and I am your partner." He said: I narrated this hadith to ‘Āmir, and he said: "He spoke the truth," and he recognized the hadith.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا حَاتِمُ بْنُ إِسْمَاعِيلَ، عَنْ جَعْفَرٍ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، قَالَ: نَذَرَتِ امْرَأَةٌ أَنْ تُقَادَ مَزْمُومَةً بِزِمَامٍ فِي أَنْفِهَا , فَوَقَعَ بَعِيرُهَا , فَانْقَطَعَ زِمَامُهَا , فَخُرِمَ أَنْفُهَا , فَأَتَتْ عَلِيًّا تَطْلُبُ حَقَّهَا , فَأَبْطَلَهُ وَقَالَ: إِنَّمَا نَذَرْتِيهِ لِلَّهِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Hatim b. Isma'il -> Ja'far -> His Father
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ḥātim ibn Ismā‘īl narrated to us, from Ja‘far, from his father, who said: A woman vowed to be led by a rein in her nose. Her camel fell, and her rein broke, tearing her nose. She came to ‘Alī seeking her right. He annulled it and said: "You only vowed it to Allah."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ بَكْرٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ جُرَيْجٍ، قَالَ: قَالَ عَطَاءٌ: "إِنْ قَتَلَ رَجُلٌ رَجُلًا خَطَأً ثُمَّ آخَرَ عَمْدًا , قَالَ: فَلْيُؤَدِّ الْخَطَأَ مِنْ أَجْلِ أَنَّهُ قَدْ ثَبَتَ عَقْلُهُ قَبْلَ الْعَمْدِ , قَالَ لَهُ إِنْسَانٌ: وَقَتَلَ عَمْدًا ثُمَّ قَتَلَ خَطَأً , قَالَ: فَلَا يُؤَدِّ مِنْ أَجْلِ أَنَّهُ قَدْ أَغْلَقَ دَمَهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Muhammad b. Bakr -> Ibn Jurayj -> 'Ata'
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Muḥammad ibn Bakr narrated to us, from Ibn Jurayj, who said: ‘Aṭā’ said: "If a man kills another man by mistake, then another intentionally, he said: Let him pay for the mistake because its blood money was established before the intentional killing. A person said to him: What if he killed intentionally then killed by mistake? He said: He does not pay because he has forfeited his blood."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ بَكْرٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ جُرَيْجٍ، قَالَ: قُلْتُ لِعَطَاءٍ: رَجُلٌ قَتَلَ رَجُلًا عَمْدًا فَفَرَّ، فَلَمْ يُقْدَرْ عَلَيْهِ حَتَّى مَاتَ وَتَرَكَ مَالًا؟ قَالَ: فَدِيَتُهُ فِي مَالِهِ دِيَةُ الْمَقْتُولِ , قِيلَ لَهُ: سُجِنَ الْقَاتِلُ حَتَّى مَاتَ , قَالَ: قَدْ قَتَلُوهُ , حَبَسُوهُ حَتَّى مَاتَ فِي السِّجْنِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Muhammad b. Bakr -> Ibn Jurayj -> 'Ata'
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Muḥammad ibn Bakr narrated to us, from Ibn Jurayj, who said: I said to ‘Aṭā’: "A man killed another man intentionally and fled, and was not caught until he died, leaving wealth?" He said: "His blood money is from his wealth, the blood money of the killed person." It was said to him: "The killer was imprisoned until he died?" He said: "They killed him; they imprisoned him until he died in prison."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مَرْوَانُ بْنُ مُعَاوِيَةَ، عَنْ صَاعِدِ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ، قَالَ: سُئِلَ عَنْ قَتِيلٍ وُجِدَ فِي ثَلَاثَةِ أَحْيَاءٍ: رَأْسُهُ فِي حَيٍّ , وَرِجْلَاهُ فِي حَيٍّ , وَوَسَطُهُ فِي حَيٍّ , قَالَ الشَّعْبِيُّ: يُصَلَّى عَلَى الْوَسَطِ , وَعَلَى أَهْلِ الْوَسَطِ الدِّيَةُ وَقَسَامَةٌ مَا قَتَلْنَا وَلَا عَلِمْنَا قَاتِلًا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Marwan b. Mu'awiyah -> Sa'id b. Muslim -> Al-Sha'bi
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Marwān ibn Mu‘āwiyah narrated to us, from Ṣā‘id ibn Muslim, from Al-Sha‘bī, who said: He was asked about a slain person found in three neighborhoods: his head in one neighborhood, his legs in another, and his torso in another. Al-Sha‘bī said: "The funeral prayer is performed over the torso, and the blood money and the oath (Qasāmah) stating 'We did not kill nor do we know a killer' are upon the people of the torso."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مُعَاوِيَةُ بْنُ هِشَامٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ، عَنْ مَعْمَرٍ، عَنْ رَجُلٍ، عَنْ عِكْرِمَةَ، قَالَ: لَيْسَ فِي دِيَةِ الدَّنَانِيرِ وَالدَّرَاهِمِ مُغَلَّظَةٌ , إِنَّمَا الْمُغَلَّظَةُ فِي الْإِبِلِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Mu'awiyah b. Hisham -> Sufyan -> Ma'mar -> A Man -> Ikrimah
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Mu‘āwiyah ibn Hishām narrated to us, saying: Sufyān narrated to us, from Ma‘mar, from a man, from ‘Ikrimah, who said: "There is no aggravation in the blood money of dinars and dirhams; aggravation is only in camels."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ مَهْدِيٍّ، عَنِ الْقَاسِمِ بْنِ الْفَضْلِ، عَنْ هَارُونَ، عَنْ عِكْرِمَةَ، فِي رَجُلٍ قَتَلَ بَعْدَ أَخْذِ الدِّيَةِ , قَالَ: "يُقْتَلُ , أَمَا سَمِعْتَ اللَّهَ يَقُولُ: {فَلَهُ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ} [البقرة: 178]

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Ibn Mahdi -> Al-Qasim b. al-Fadl -> Harun -> Ikrimah
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ibn Mahdī narrated to us, from Al-Qāsim ibn al-Faḍl, from Hārūn, from ‘Ikrimah, regarding a man who kills after accepting the blood money. He said: "He is killed. Did you not hear Allah say: '{For him is a painful punishment}' [Al-Baqarah: 178]."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ مَهْدِيٍّ، عَنْ حَمَّادِ بْنِ سَلَمَةَ، عَنْ يُونُسَ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، فِيمَنْ قَتَلَ بَعْدَ أَخْذِ الدِّيَةِ , قَالَ: يُؤْخَذُ مِنْهُ الدِّيَةُ وَلَا يُقْتَلُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Ibn Mahdi -> Hammad b. Salamah -> Yunus -> Al-Hasan
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ibn Mahdī narrated to us, from Ḥammād ibn Salamah, from Yūnus, from Al-Ḥasan, regarding one who kills after accepting the blood money. He said: "Blood money is taken from him, and he is not killed."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو دَاوُدَ، عَنْ وَهْبٍ، عَنْ يُونُسَ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، فِي رَجُلٍ قُتِلَ لَهُ قَتِيلٌ فَعَفَا عَنْهُ , ثُمَّ رَاحَ فَقَتَلَهُ , قَالَ الْحَسَنُ: لَا يُقْتَلُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Abu Dawud -> Wahb -> Yunus -> Al-Hasan
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Abū Dāwūd narrated to us, from Wahb, from Yūnus, from Al-Ḥasan, regarding a man whose relative was killed and he pardoned him (the killer), then went and killed him. Al-Ḥasan said: "He is not killed."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا حُمَيْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ، عَنْ زُهَيْرٍ، عَنْ جَابِرٍ، فِي امْرَأَةٍ حَبِلَتْ مِنَ الزِّنَا، فَحُبِسَتْ لِتَضَعَ مَا فِي بَطْنِهَا ثُمَّ تُرْجَمُ , فَدَخَلَ عَلَيْهَا رَجُلٌ فَقَتَلَهَا , قَالَ: قَالَ عَامِرٌ: لَا أَعْلَمُ فِيهَا شَيْئًا غَيْرَ أَنَّ الْوَلَدَ لِلسُّلْطَانِ يَحْكُمُ فِيهِ مَا شَاءَ قَالَ: وَحَدَّثَنِي حَمَّادٌ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: لَيْسَ أَحَدٌ مِنَ الْمُسْلِمِينَ بِأَحَقَّ بِهَا بَعْضُهُمْ مِنْ بَعْضٍ , وَقَالَ حَمَّادٌ: فِي الْوَلَدِ غُرَّةٌ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Humayd b. Abd al-Rahman -> Zuhayr -> Jabir
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ḥumayd ibn ‘Abd al-Raḥmān narrated to us, from Zuhayr, from Jābir, regarding a woman who became pregnant from adultery and was imprisoned to give birth before being stoned, then a man entered upon her and killed her. He said: ‘Āmir said: "I do not know anything about it except that the child belongs to the ruler to decide regarding it whatever he wishes." He said: And Ḥammād narrated to me from Ibrāhīm, who said: "None of the Muslims has more right to her than another." And Ḥammād said: "There is a Ghurrah for the child."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا حُمَيْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ، عَنْ حَسَنٍ، عَنْ جَابِرٍ، عَنْ عَامِرٍ، فِي صَاحِبِ الْمَعْبَرِ يَعْبُرُ بِدَوَابَّ فَغَرِقَتْ , قَالَ: لَا ضَمَانَ عَلَيْهِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Humayd b. Abd al-Rahman -> Hasan -> Jabir -> 'Amir
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ḥumayd ibn ‘Abd al-Raḥmān narrated to us, from Ḥasan, from Jābir, from ‘Āmir, regarding a ferry owner who transports animals and they drown. He said: "He is not liable."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الرَّحِيمِ، وَابْنُ نُمَيْرٍ، عَنْ حَجَّاجٍ، عَنْ مَكْحُولٍ، عَنْ زَيْدِ بْنِ ثَابِتٍ، قَالَ: فِي شَحْمَةِ الْأُذُنِ ثُلُثُ دِيَةِ الْأُذُنِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Abd al-Rahim / Ibn Numayr -> Hajjaj -> Makhul -> Zayd b. Thabit
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: ‘Abd al-Raḥīm and Ibn Numayr narrated to us, from Ḥajjāj, from Makḥūl, from Zayd ibn Thābit, who said: "In the earlobe is one-third of the blood money for the ear."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ إِدْرِيسَ، عَنْ حُصَيْنٍ، عَنْ عَامِرٍ، قَالَ: اخْتُصِمَ إِلَى عَلِيٍّ فِي ثَوْرٍ نَطَحَ حِمَارًا، فَقَتَلَهُ فَقَالَ عَلِيٌّ: إِنْ كَانَ الثَّوْرُ دَخَلَ عَلَى الْحِمَارِ، فَقَتَلَهُ فَقَدْ ضَمِنَ , وَإِنْ كَانَ الْحِمَارُ دَخَلَ عَلَى الثَّوْرِ فَقَتَلَهُ، فَلَا ضَمَانَ عَلَيْهِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Abd Allah b. Idris -> Husayn -> 'Amir
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: ‘Abd Allāh ibn Idrīs narrated to us, from Ḥuṣayn, from ‘Āmir, who said: A dispute was brought to ‘Alī regarding a bull that gored a donkey and killed it. ‘Alī said: "If the bull entered upon the donkey and killed it, he is liable. But if the donkey entered upon the bull and he killed it, he is not liable."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا شَرِيكٌ، عَنْ جَابِرٍ، عَنْ عَامِرٍ، قَالَ: يُقْتَصُّ لِبَعْضِهِمْ مِنْ بَعْضٍ، ثُمَّ تُقَامُ الْحُدُودُ يَعْنِي فِي الْقَوْمِ يَجْرَحُ بَعْضُهُمْ بَعْضًا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Sharik -> Jabir -> 'Amir
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Sharīk narrated to us, from Jābir, from ‘Āmir, who said: "Retaliation is taken for some of them from others, then the prescribed punishments are carried out." Meaning regarding a people who wound each other.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو مُحَمَّدٍ عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ يُونُسَ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا بَقِيُّ بْنُ مَخْلَدٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِي شَيْبَةَ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا حَفْصُ بْنُ غِيَاثٍ، عَنْ جَعْفَرٍ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ: أَنَّ النَّبِيَّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ قَالَ لِأُسَامَةَ: يَا أُسَامَةُ، لَا تَشْفَعْ فِي حَدٍّ وَكَانَ إِذَا شَفَعَ شَفَّعَهُ

english.narrator: Abu Muhammad Abd Allah b. Yunus -> Baqi b. Makhlad -> Abd Allah b. Muhammad b. Abi Shaybah -> Hafs b. Ghiyath -> Ja'far -> His Father
english.text: Abu Muhammad ‘Abd Allāh ibn Yūnus narrated to us, saying: Baqī ibn Makhlad narrated to us, saying: ‘Abd Allāh ibn Muḥammad ibn Abī Shaybah narrated to us, saying: Ḥafṣ ibn Ghiyāth narrated to us, from Ja‘far, from his father: That the Prophet ﷺ said to Usāmah: "O Usāmah, do not intercede in a prescribed punishment (Ḥadd)." And whenever he interceded, he would accept his intercession.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ واصِلٍ، عَنْ أَبِي وَائِلٍ، عَنْ كَعْبٍ، قَالَ: لَا تَشْفَعْ فِي حَدٍّ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Waki' -> Sufyan -> Wasil -> Abu Wa'il -> Ka'b
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Wakī‘ narrated to us, from Sufyān, from Wāṣil, from Abū Wā’il, from Ka‘b, who said: "Do not intercede in a prescribed punishment."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ هِشَامِ بْنِ عُرْوَةَ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عُرْوَةَ، عَنِ الْفُرَافِصَةِ الْحَنَفِيِّ، قَالَ: مَرُّوا عَلَى الزُّبَيْرِ بِسَارِقٍ فَتَشَفَّعَ لَهُ، قَالُوا: أَتَشْفَعُ لِسَارِقٍ؟ قَالَ: نَعَمْ، مَا لَمْ يُؤْتَ بِهِ إِلَى الْإِمَامِ، فَإِذَا أُتِيَ بِهِ إِلَى الْإِمَامِ، فَلَا عَفَا اللَّهُ عَنْهُ إِنْ عَفَا عَنْهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Waki' -> Hisham b. Urwah -> Abd Allah b. Urwah -> Al-Furafisah al-Hanafi
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Wakī‘ narrated to us, from Hishām ibn ‘Urwah, from ‘Abd Allāh ibn ‘Urwah, from Al-Furāfiṣah al-Ḥanafī, who said: They passed by Al-Zubayr with a thief, and he interceded for him. They said: "Do you intercede for a thief?" He said: "Yes, as long as he has not been brought to the Imam. But if he is brought to the Imam, may Allah not pardon him if he pardons him."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا حُمَيْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ، عَنْ هِشَامٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عُرْوَةَ، عَنِ الْفُرَافِصَةِ، عَنِ الزُّبَيْرِ، مِثْلَهُ

english.narrator: Humayd b. Abd al-Rahman -> Hisham -> Abd Allah b. Urwah -> Al-Furafisah -> Al-Zubayr
english.text: Ḥumayd ibn ‘Abd al-Raḥmān narrated to us, from Hishām, from ‘Abd Allāh ibn ‘Urwah, from Al-Furāfiṣah, from Al-Zubayr, similar to it.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا حُمَيْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ الرُّوَاسِيُّ، عَنْ هِشَامٍ، عَنْ أَبِي حَازِمٍ: أَنَّ عَلِيًّا شَفَعَ لِسَارِقٍ فَقِيلَ لَهُ: تَشْفَعُ لِسَارِقٍ؟، قَالَ: نَعَمْ، إِنَّ ذَلِكَ يُفْعَلُ مَا لَمْ يَبْلُغِ الْإِمَامَ، فَإِذَا بَلَغَ الْإِمَامَ، فَلَا أَعْفَاهُ اللَّهُ إِذَا عَفَاهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Humayd b. Abd al-Rahman al-Ru'asi -> Hisham -> Abu Hazim
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ḥumayd ibn ‘Abd al-Raḥmān al-Ru’āsī narrated to us, from Hishām, from Abū Ḥāzim: That ‘Alī interceded for a thief. It was said to him: "Do you intercede for a thief?" He said: "Yes, that is done as long as it has not reached the Imam. But if it reaches the Imam, may Allah not pardon him if he pardons him."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ عُبَيْدٍ، عَنْ سُلَيْمَانَ بْنِ أَبِي كَبْشَةَ: أَنَّ سَارِقًا مُرَّ بِهِ عَلَى سَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَيْرٍ وَعَطَاءٍ فَشَفَعَا لَهُ فَقِيلَ لَهُمَا: وَتَرَيَانِ ذَلِكَ؟ فَقَالَا: نَعَمْ، مَا لَمْ يُؤْتَ بِهِ إِلَى الْإِمَامِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Waki' -> Sa'id b. Ubayd -> Sulayman b. Abi Kabshah
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Wakī‘ narrated to us, from Sa‘īd ibn ‘Ubayd, from Sulaymān ibn Abī Kabshah: That a thief was passed by Sa‘īd ibn Jubayr and ‘Aṭā’, and they interceded for him. It was said to them: "Do you see that as permissible?" They said: "Yes, as long as he has not been brought to the Imam."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدَةُ، عَنْ يَحْيَى بْنِ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الْوَهَّابِ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ، قَالَ: مَنْ حَالَتْ شَفَاعَتُهُ دُونَ حَدٍّ مِنْ حُدُودِ اللَّهِ، فَقَدْ ضَادَّ اللَّهَ فِي خَلْقِهِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> 'Abdah -> Yahya b. Sa'id -> Abd al-Wahhab -> Ibn Umar
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: ‘Abdah narrated to us, from Yaḥyā ibn Sa‘īd, from ‘Abd al-Wahhāb, from Ibn ‘Umar, who said: "Whoever's intercession prevents a prescribed punishment of Allah, he has opposed Allah regarding His creation."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ عُيَيْنَةَ، عَنِ الزُّهْرِيِّ، عَنْ عُرْوَةَ، عَنْ عَائِشَةَ: أَنَّ النَّبِيَّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ كُلِّمَ فِي شَيْءٍ، قَالَ: لَوْ كَانَتْ فَاطِمَةَ ابْنَةَ مُحَمَّدٍ لَأَقَمْتُ عَلَيْهَا الْحَدَّ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Ibn Uyaynah -> Al-Zuhri -> Urwah -> A'ishah
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ibn ‘Uyaynah narrated to us, from Al-Zuhrī, from ‘Urwah, from ‘Ā’ishah: That the Prophet ﷺ was spoken to regarding something. He said: "If it were Fāṭimah bint Muḥammad, I would have carried out the punishment on her."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ نُمَيْرٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ إِسْحَاقَ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ طَلْحَةَ، عَنْ أُمِّهِ عَائِشَةَ بِنْتِ مَسْعُودِ بْنِ الْأَسْوَدِ، عَنْ أَبِيهَا مَسْعُودٍ، قَالَ: لَمَّا سَرَقَتِ الْمَرْأَةُ تِلْكَ الْقَطِيفَةَ مِنْ بَيْتِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ أَعْظَمْنَا ذَلِكَ، وَكَانَتِ الْمَرْأَةُ مِنْ قُرَيْشٍ، فَجِئْنَا إِلَى النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ نُكَلِّمُهُ وَقُلْنَا: نَحْنُ نَفْدِيهَا بِأَرْبَعِينَ أُوقِيَّةً، قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: تَطَّهَّرُ خَيْرٌ لَهَا فَلَمَّا سَمِعْنَا لِينَ قَوْلِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ، أَتَيْنَا أُسَامَةَ فَقُلْنَا: كَلِّمْ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ، فَلَمَّا رَأَى رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ذَلِكَ قَامَ خَطِيبًا، قَالَ: مَا إِكْثَارُكُمْ عَلَيَّ فِي حَدٍّ مِنْ حُدُودِ اللَّهِ وَقَعَ عَلَى أَمَةٍ مِنْ إِمَاءِ اللَّهِ، وَالَّذِي نَفْسِي بِيَدِهِ، لَوْ كَانَتْ فَاطِمَةُ بِنْتُ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ نَزَلَتْ بِالَّذِي نَزَلَتْ بِهِ لَقَطَعَ مُحَمَّدٌ يَدَهَا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Ibn Numayr -> Muhammad b. Ishaq -> Muhammad b. Talhah -> His Mother A'ishah bint Mas'ud b. al-Aswad -> Her Father Mas'ud
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ibn Numayr narrated to us, saying: Muḥammad ibn Isḥāq narrated to us, from Muḥammad ibn Ṭalḥah, from his mother ‘Ā’ishah bint Mas‘ūd ibn al-Aswad, from her father Mas‘ūd, who said: When the woman stole that velvet garment from the house of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, we considered it a grave matter, and the woman was from Quraysh. So we came to the Prophet ﷺ to speak to him, and we said: "We will ransom her for forty Uqiyahs." The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Purification is better for her." When we heard the leniency in the statement of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, we went to Usāmah and said: "Speak to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ." When the Messenger of Allah ﷺ saw that, he stood up to deliver a sermon and said: "Why do you persist with me regarding a prescribed punishment of Allah that has befallen a female slave among the slaves of Allah? By the One in Whose hand is my soul, if Fāṭimah bint Rasūlillāh ﷺ had committed what she committed, Muḥammad would have cut off her hand."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ مَهْدِيٍّ، عَنِ الْحَارِثِ بْنِ شَدَّادٍ، عَنْ يَحْيَى بْنِ أَبِي كَثِيرٍ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ ثَوْبَانَ، عَنْ زُبَيْدِ بْنِ الصَّلْتِ، قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ أَبَا بَكْرٍ الصِّدِّيقَ، يَقُولُ: لَوْ أَخَذْتُ شَارِبًا لَأَحْبَبْتُ أَنْ يَسْتُرَهُ اللَّهُ، وَلَوْ أَخَذْتُ سَارِقًا لَأَحْبَبْتُ أَنْ يَسْتُرَهُ اللَّهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Abd al-Rahman b. Mahdi -> Al-Harith b. Shaddad -> Yahya b. Abi Kathir -> Muhammad b. Abd al-Rahman b. Thawban -> Zubayd b. al-Salt
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: ‘Abd al-Raḥmān ibn Mahdī narrated to us, from Al-Ḥārith ibn Shaddād, from Yaḥyā ibn Abī Kathīr, from Muḥammad ibn ‘Abd al-Raḥmān ibn Thawbān, from Zubayd ibn al-Ṣalt, who said: I heard Abū Bakr al-Ṣiddīq say: "If I caught a drinker, I would love for Allah to cover him; and if I caught a thief, I would love for Allah to cover him."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا شَرِيكٌ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ مَسْرُوقٍ، عَنْ عِكْرِمَةَ، قَالَ: سُرِقَتْ عَيْبَةٌ لِعَمَّارٍ بِالْمُزْدَلِفَةِ، فَوَضَعَ فِي أَثَرِهَا حِقَّتَهُ، وَدَعَا الْقَافَةَ، فَقَالُوا: حَبَشِيٌّ، وَاتَّبَعُوا أَثَرَهُ حَتَّى انْتَهَى إِلَى حَائِطٍ وَهُوَ يُقَلِّبُهَا، فَأَخَذَهَا وَتَرَكَهُ، فَقِيلَ لَهُ، قَالَ: أَسْتُرُ عَلَيْهِ لَعَلَّ اللَّهَ أَنْ يَسْتُرَ عَلَيَّ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Sharik -> Sa'id b. Masruq -> Ikrimah
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Sharīk narrated to us, from Sa‘īd ibn Masrūq, from ‘Ikrimah, who said: A leather bag belonging to ‘Ammār was stolen at Muzdalifah. He put his she-camel on its trail and called the trackers. They said: "An Abyssinian." They followed his trail until they reached a garden where he was turning it over. He took it and left him. It was said to him, so he said: "I cover him so that perhaps Allah will cover me."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو مُعَاوِيَةَ، عَنْ عَاصِمٍ، عَنْ عِكْرِمَةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، وعَمَّارٍ، وَالزُّبَيْرِ، أَخَذُوا سَارِقًا فَخَلَّوْا سَبِيلَهُ، فَقُلْتُ لِابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ: بِئْسَمَا صَنَعْتُمْ حِينَ خَلَّيْتُمْ سَبِيلَهُ، قَالَ: لَا أُمَّ لَكَ، أَمَّا لَوْ كُنْتَ أَنْتَ لَسَرَّكَ أَنْ يُخَلَّى سَبِيلُكَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Abu Mu'awiyah -> Asim -> Ikrimah -> Ibn Abbas / Ammar / Al-Zubayr
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Abū Mu‘āwiyah narrated to us, from ‘Āṣim, from ‘Ikrimah, from Ibn ‘Abbās, ‘Ammār, and Al-Zubayr, that they caught a thief and let him go. I said to Ibn ‘Abbās: "You did badly when you let him go." He said: "May you be bereft of your mother! If it were you, you would have been pleased to be let go."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَلِيُّ بْنُ مُسْهِرٍ، عَنْ عُبَيْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عُمَرَ، عَنْ نَافِعٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ، قَالَ: قَطَعَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ فِي مِجَنٍّ ثَمَنُهُ ثَلَاثَةُ دَرَاهِمَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Ali b. Mushir -> Ubayd Allah b. Umar -> Nafi' -> Ibn Umar
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: ‘Alī ibn Mushir narrated to us, from ‘Ubayd Allāh ibn ‘Umar, from Nāfi‘, from Ibn ‘Umar, who said: "The Messenger of Allah ﷺ cut off the hand for a shield whose price was three dirhams."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا يَزِيدُ بْنُ هَارُونَ، قَالَ: أَخْبَرَنَا سُلَيْمَانُ بْنُ كَثِيرٍ، وَإِبْرَاهِيمُ بْنُ سَعْدٍ، قَالَا جَمِيعًا: أَخْبَرَنَا الزُّهْرِيُّ، عَنْ عُرْوَةَ، عَنْ عَائِشَةَ، عَنِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ، قَالَ: الْقَطْعُ فِي رُبْعِ دِينَارٍ فَصَاعِدًا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Yazid b. Harun -> Sulayman b. Kathir / Ibrahim b. Sa'd -> Al-Zuhri -> Urwah -> A'ishah -> The Prophet ﷺ
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Yazīd ibn Hārūn narrated to us, saying: Sulaymān ibn Kathīr and Ibrāhīm ibn Sa‘d informed us, both saying: Al-Zuhrī informed us, from ‘Urwah, from ‘Ā’ishah, from the Prophet ﷺ, who said: "Cutting off the hand is for a quarter of a dinar and above."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ مَهْدِيٍّ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ عِيسَى بْنِ أَبِي عَزَّةَ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ: أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ قَطَعَ فِي خَمْسَةِ دَرَاهِمَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Ibn Mahdi -> Sufyan -> Isa b. Abi 'Azzah -> Al-Sha'bi -> Abd Allah
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ibn Mahdī narrated to us, from Sufyān, from ‘Īsā ibn Abī ‘Azzah, from Al-Sha‘bī, from ‘Abd Allāh: "That the Messenger of Allah ﷺ cut off the hand for five dirhams."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ إِسْحَاقَ، عَنْ وهَيْبٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو وَاقِدٍ، عَنْ عَامِرِ بْنِ سَعْدٍ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ، قَالَ: يُقْطَعُ السَّارِقُ فِي ثَمَنِ الْمِجَنِّ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Ahmad b. Ishaq -> Wuhayb -> Abu Waqid -> Amir b. Sa'd -> His Father -> The Prophet ﷺ
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Aḥmad ibn Isḥāq narrated to us, from Wuhayb, who said: Abū Wāqid narrated to us, from ‘Āmir ibn Sa‘d, from his father, from the Prophet ﷺ, who said: "The thief's hand is cut off for the price of a shield."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا حَاتِمُ بْنُ إِسْمَاعِيلَ، عَنْ جَعْفَرٍ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنْ عَلِيٍّ: أَنَّهُ قَطَعَ يَدَ سَارِقٍ فِي بَيْضَةِ حَدِيدٍ ثَمَنُهَا رُبْعُ دِينَارٍ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Hatim b. Isma'il -> Ja'far -> His Father -> Ali
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ḥātim ibn Ismā‘īl narrated to us, from Ja‘far, from his father, from ‘Alī: "That he cut off the hand of a thief for an iron helmet whose price was a quarter of a dinar."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الرَّحِيمِ بْنُ سُلَيْمَانَ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ إِسْحَاقَ، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ شُعَيْبٍ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنْ جَدِّهِ، قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ النَّبِيَّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ، يَقُولُ: الْقَطْعُ فِي ثَمَنِ الْمِجَنِّ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Abd al-Rahim b. Sulayman -> Muhammad b. Ishaq -> Amr b. Shu'ayb -> His Father -> His Grandfather -> The Prophet ﷺ
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: ‘Abd al-Raḥīm ibn Sulaymān narrated to us, from Muḥammad ibn Isḥāq, from ‘Amr ibn Shu‘ayb, from his father, from his grandfather, who said: I heard the Prophet ﷺ say: "Cutting off the hand is for the price of a shield."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الرَّحِيمِ، عَنْ يَحْيَى بْنِ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ عَمْرَةَ، عَنْ عَائِشَةَ أُمِّ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ، قَالَتْ: الْقَطْعُ فِي رُبْعِ دِينَارٍ فَصَاعِدًا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Abd al-Rahim -> Yahya b. Sa'id -> Amrah -> A'ishah
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: ‘Abd al-Raḥīm narrated to us, from Yaḥyā ibn Sa‘īd, from ‘Amrah, from ‘Ā’ishah, the Mother of the Believers, who said: "Cutting off the hand is for a quarter of a dinar and above."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مَرْوَانُ بْنُ مُعَاوِيَةَ، عَنْ حُمَيْدٍ، قَالَ: سُئِلَ أَنَسٌ فِي كَمْ يُقْطَعُ يَدُ السَّارِقِ؟، قَالَ: قَدْ قَطَعَ أَبُو بَكْرٍ فِيمَا لَا يَسُرُّنِي أَنَّهُ لِي بِخَمْسَةِ دَرَاهِمَ أَوْ ثَلَاثَةِ دَرَاهِمَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Marwan b. Mu'awiyah -> Humayd -> Anas
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Marwān ibn Mu‘āwiyah narrated to us, from Ḥumayd, who said: Anas was asked: "For how much is the hand of a thief cut off?" He said: "Abu Bakr cut off the hand for something I would not be pleased to own for five dirhams or three dirhams."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، عَنْ أَنَسٍ: أَنَّ رَجُلًا سَرَقَ مِجَنًّا عَلَى عَهْدِ أَبِي بَكْرٍ فَقُطِعَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Waki' -> Shu'bah -> Qatadah -> Anas
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Wakī‘ narrated to us, from Shu‘bah, from Qatādah, from Anas: "That a man stole a shield during the time of Abu Bakr, and his hand was cut off."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْوَهَّابِ الثَّقَفِيُّ، عَنْ خَالِدٍ، عَنْ عِكْرِمَةَ، قَالَ: تُقْطَعُ الْيَدُ فِي ثَمَنِ الْمِجَنِّ، قَالَ: قُلْتُ لَهُ: ذَكَرَ لَكَ ثَمَنَهُ؟، قَالَ: أَرْبَعَةٌ أَوْ خَمْسَةٌ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Abd al-Wahhab al-Thaqafi -> Khalid -> Ikrimah
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: ‘Abd al-Wahhāb al-Thaqafī narrated to us, from Khālid, from ‘Ikrimah, who said: "The hand is cut off for the price of a shield." He said: I said to him: "Did he mention its price to you?" He said: "Four or five."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا غُنْدَرٌ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، عَنْ دَاوُدَ بْنِ فَرَاهِيجَ، أَنَّهُ سَمِعَ أَبَا هُرَيْرَةَ، وأَبَا سَعِيدٍ الْخُدْرِيِّ، يَقُولَانِ: لَا تُقْطَعُ الْيَدُ إِلَّا فِي أَرْبَعَةِ دَرَاهِمَ فَصَاعِدًا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Ghundar -> Shu'bah -> Dawud b. Farahij -> Abu Hurayrah / Abu Sa'id al-Khudri
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ghundar narrated to us, from Shu‘bah, from Dāwūd ibn Farāhīj, that he heard Abū Hurayrah and Abū Sa‘īd al-Khudrī saying: "The hand is not cut off except for four dirhams and above."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ عُيَيْنَةَ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ أَبِي بَكْرٍ، عَنْ عَمْرَةَ، قَالَتْ: قَدْ عَلِمْتُ أَنَّ عُثْمَانَ قَطَعَ فِي أُتْرُجَّةٍ قُوِّمَتْ ثَلَاثَةَ دَرَاهِمَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Ibn Uyaynah -> Abd Allah b. Abi Bakr -> Amrah
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ibn ‘Uyaynah narrated to us, from ‘Abd Allāh ibn Abī Bakr, from ‘Amrah, who said: "I knew that ‘Uthmān cut off the hand for a citron valued at three dirhams."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ أَبِي بَكْرٍ، عَنْ عَمْرَةَ، عَنْ عَائِشَةَ، قَالَتْ: تُقْطَعُ فِي رُبْعِ دِينَارٍ وَقَالَتْ عَمْرَةُ: قَطَعَ عُمَرُ فِي أُتْرُجَّةٍ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Waki' -> Sufyan -> Abd Allah b. Abi Bakr -> Amrah -> A'ishah
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Wakī‘ narrated to us, from Sufyān, from ‘Abd Allāh ibn Abī Bakr, from ‘Amrah, from ‘Ā’ishah, who said: "It is cut off for a quarter of a dinar." And ‘Amrah said: "‘Umar cut off the hand for a citron."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْأَعْلَى، عَنْ بُرْدٍ، عَنْ مَكْحُولٍ، قَالَ: يُقْطَعُ السَّارِقُ فِي ثَمَنِ الْمِجَنِّ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Abd al-A'la -> Burd -> Makhul
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: ‘Abd al-A‘lā narrated to us, from Burd, from Makḥūl, who said: "The thief's hand is cut off for the price of a shield."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ إِدْرِيسَ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي عَرُوبَةَ، وإِسْمَاعِيلُ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ الْمُسَيِّبِ، عَنْ عُمَرَ، قَالَ: لَا تُقْطَعُ الْخَمْسُ إِلَّا فِي خَمْسٍ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Ibn Idris -> Ibn Abi Arubah / Isma'il -> Qatadah -> Sa'id b. al-Musayyib -> Umar
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ibn Idrīs narrated to us, from Ibn Abī ‘Arūbah and Ismā‘īl, from Qatādah, from Sa‘īd ibn al-Musayyib, from ‘Umar, who said: "The five (fingers/hand) are not cut off except for five (dirhams)."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا يَحْيَى بْنُ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ الْقَاسِمِ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ: أَنَّ ابْنَ الزُّبَيْرِ قَطَعَ فِي نَعْلَيْنِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Yahya b. Sa'id -> Abd al-Rahman b. al-Qasim -> His Father
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Yaḥyā ibn Sa‘īd narrated to us, from ‘Abd al-Raḥmān ibn al-Qāsim, from his father: "That Ibn al-Zubayr cut off the hand for two sandals."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو أُسَامَةَ، عَنْ عُبَيْدِ اللَّهِ، عَنْ نَافِعٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ، قَالَ: كَانُوا يَتَسَارَقُونَ السِّيَاطَ فِي طَرِيقِ مَكَّةَ فَقَالَ عُثْمَانُ: لَئِنْ عُدْتُمْ لَأَقْطَعَنَّ فِيهِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Abu Usamah -> 'Ubayd Allah -> Nafi' -> Ibn 'Umar
english.text: Ibn Umar said: "They used to steal whips on the road to Mecca." Uthman said: "If you return [to doing this], I will surely cut [the hand] for it."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو مُعَاوِيَةَ، عَنِ الْأَعْمَشِ، عَنْ أَبِي صَالِحٍ، عَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ، قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: لَعَنَ اللَّهُ السَّارِقَ يَسْرِقُ الْبَيْضَةَ فَتُقْطَعُ يَدُهُ، وَيَسْرِقُ الْحَبْلَ فَتُقْطَعُ يَدُهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Abu Mu'awiyah -> Al-A'mash -> Abu Salih -> Abu Hurayrah
english.text: Abu Hurayrah reported that the Messenger of Allah (saw) said: "May Allah curse the thief who steals an egg and has his hand cut off, and steals a rope and has his hand cut off."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الرَّحِيمِ، عَنْ يَحْيَى بْنِ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ أَبِي بَكْرِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ، قَالَ: أُتِيَ عُثْمَانُ بِرَجُلٍ سَرَقَ أُتْرُجَّةً فَقَوَّمَهَا رُبْعَ دِينَارٍ، فَقَطَعَ يَدَهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> 'Abd al-Rahim -> Yahya ibn Sa'id -> Abu Bakr ibn Muhammad
english.text: Abu Bakr ibn Muhammad said: "A man was brought to Uthman who had stolen a citron. He valued it at a quarter of a dinar, so he cut off his hand."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْأَعْلَى، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ إِسْحَاقَ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنِي أَيُّوبُ بْنُ مُوسَى، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ: لَا يُقْطَعُ السَّارِقُ فِي دُونِ ثَمَنِ الْمِجَنِّ، وَثَمَنُ الْمِجَنِّ عَشَرَةُ دَرَاهِمَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> 'Abd al-A'la -> Muhammad ibn Ishaq -> Ayyub ibn Musa -> 'Ata' -> Ibn 'Abbas
english.text: Ibn Abbas said: "The thief's hand is not cut off for less than the price of a shield, and the price of a shield is ten dirhams."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْأَعْلَى، وَعَبْدُ الرَّحِيمِ بْنُ سُلَيْمَانَ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ إِسْحَاقَ، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ شُعَيْبٍ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنْ جَدِّهِ، قَالَ: كَانَ يَقُولُ: ثَمَنُ الْمِجَنِّ عَشَرَةُ دَرَاهِمَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> 'Abd al-A'la and 'Abd al-Rahim ibn Sulayman -> Muhammad ibn Ishaq -> 'Amr ibn Shu'ayb -> His father -> His grandfather
english.text: 'Amr ibn Shu'ayb reported from his father, from his grandfather, who used to say: "The price of a shield is ten dirhams."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ مُبَارَكٍ، وَوَكِيعٌ، عَنِ الْمَسْعُودِيِّ، عَنِ الْقَاسِمِ، عَنِ ابْنِ مَسْعُودٍ، أَنَّهُ، قَالَ: لَا يُقْطَعُ إِلَّا فِي دِينَارٍ أَوْ عَشَرَةِ دَرَاهِمَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Ibn Mubarak and Waki' -> Al-Mas'udi -> Al-Qasim -> Ibn Mas'ud
english.text: Ibn Mas'ud said: "[The hand] is not cut off except for a dinar or ten dirhams."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ حَمْزَةَ الزَّيَّاتِ، عَنِ الْحَكَمِ، عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ، قَالَ: قِيمَةُ الْمِجَنِّ دِينَارٌ، الَّذِي يُقْطَعُ فِيهِ الْيَدُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Waki' -> Hamzah al-Zayyat -> Al-Hakam -> Abu Ja'far
english.text: Abu Ja'far said: "The value of the shield for which the hand is cut off is a dinar."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الرَّحِيمِ بْنُ سُلَيْمَانَ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الْمَلِكِ بْنِ أَبِي سُلَيْمَانَ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، قَالَ: أَدْنَى مَا يُقْطَعُ فِيهِ السَّارِقُ ثَمَنُ الْمِجَنِّ، وَكَانَ يُقَوَّمُ الْمِجَنُّ فِي زَمَانِهِمْ دِينَارًا أَوْ عَشَرَةَ دَرَاهِمَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> 'Abd al-Rahim ibn Sulayman -> 'Abd al-Malik ibn Abi Sulayman -> 'Ata'
english.text: Ata said: "The minimum for which a thief's hand is cut off is the price of a shield, and the shield was valued in their time at a dinar or ten dirhams."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ حَمَّادٍ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: قَالَ عَبْدُ اللَّهِ: لَا تُقْطَعُ الْيَدُ إِلَّا فِي تُرْسٍ أَوْ حَجَفَةٍ، قَالَ: قُلْتُ لِإِبْرَاهِيمَ: كَمْ قِيمَتُهُ؟، قَالَ: دِينَارٌ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Waki' -> Sufyan -> Hammad -> Ibrahim
english.text: Abdullah said: "The hand is not cut off except for a shield or buckler." I asked Ibrahim: "What is its value?" He said: "A dinar."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ هِشَامِ بْنِ عُرْوَةَ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، قَالَ: كَانَ السَّارِقُ عَلَى عَهْدِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ يُقْطَعُ فِي ثَمَنِ الْمِجَنِّ، وَكَانَ الْمِجَنُّ يَوْمَئِذٍ لَهُ ثَمَنٌ، وَلَمْ يَكُنْ يُقْطَعُ فِي الشَّيْءِ التَّافِهِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Waki' -> Hisham ibn 'Urwah -> His father
english.text: Urwah ibn al-Zubayr said: "The thief during the time of the Prophet (saw) used to have his hand cut off for the price of a shield, and the shield in those days had value. The hand was not cut off for something insignificant."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ بَكْرٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ جُرَيْجٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ طَاوُسٍ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، قَالَ: يُقْطَعُ فِي ثَمَنِ الْمِجَنِّ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Muhammad ibn Bakr -> Ibn Jurayj -> Ibn Tawus -> His father
english.text: Tawus said: "[The hand] is cut off for the price of a shield."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا شَرِيكٌ، عَنْ عَطِيَّةَ بْنِ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ، عَنِ الْقَاسِمِ، قَالَ: أُتِيَ عُمَرُ بِسَارِقٍ فَأَمَرَ بِقَطْعِهِ، قَالَ عُثْمَانُ: إِنَّ سَرِقَتَهُ لَا تُسَاوِي عَشَرَةَ دَرَاهِمَ، قَالَ: فَأَمَرَ بِهِ عُمَرُ فَقُوِّمَتْ ثَمَانِيَةَ دَرَاهِمَ فَلَمْ يَقْطَعْهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Sharik -> 'Atiyyah ibn 'Abd al-Rahman -> Al-Qasim
english.text: Al-Qasim reported that a thief was brought to Umar, and he ordered his hand cut off. Uthman said: "What he stole is not worth ten dirhams." Umar ordered it to be valued, and it was valued at eight dirhams, so he did not cut off his hand.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا الثَّقَفِيُّ، عَنِ الْمُثَنَّى، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ شُعَيْبٍ، قَالَ: دَخَلْتُ عَلَى سَعِيدِ بْنِ الْمُسَيِّبِ فَقُلْتُ لَهُ: إِنَّ أَصْحَابَكَ عُرْوَةَ بْنَ الزُّبَيْرِ، ومُحَمَّدَ بْنَ مُسْلِمٍ الزُّهْرِيَّ، وَابْنَ يَسَارٍ، يَقُولُونَ: ثَمَنُ الْمِجَنِّ خَمْسَةُ دَرَاهِمَ، قَالَ: أَمَّا هَذَا فَقَدْ مَضَتْ فِيهِ سُنَّةُ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ عَشَرَةُ دَرَاهِمَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Al-Thaqafi -> Al-Muthanna -> 'Amr ibn Shu'ayb
english.text: 'Amr ibn Shu'ayb said: I entered upon Sa'id ibn al-Musayyib and said to him: "Your companions Urwah ibn al-Zubayr, Muhammad ibn Muslim al-Zuhri, and Ibn Yasar say that the price of a shield is five dirhams." He said: "As for this, the Sunnah of the Messenger of Allah (saw) established it as ten dirhams."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الرَّحِيمِ بْنُ سُلَيْمَانَ، عَنْ هِشَامِ بْنِ عُرْوَةَ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنْ عَائِشَةَ، قَالَتْ: لَمْ يَكُنْ يُقْطَعُ عَلَى عَهْدِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ فِي الشَّيْءِ التَّافِهِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> 'Abd al-Rahim ibn Sulayman -> Hisham ibn 'Urwah -> His father -> 'Aisha
english.text: Aisha said: "[The hand] was not cut off during the time of the Prophet (saw) for something insignificant."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنِ ابْنِ جُرَيْجٍ، عَنْ سُلَيْمَانَ بْنِ مُوسَى، عَنْ عُثْمَانَ، قَالَ: لَيْسَ عَلَيْهِ قَطْعٌ حَتَّى يَخْرُجَ بِالْمَتَاعِ مِنَ الْبَيْتِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Waki' -> Ibn Jurayj -> Sulayman ibn Musa -> 'Uthman
english.text: Uthman said: "There is no cutting [of the hand] until he takes the goods out of the house."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنِ ابْنِ جُرَيْجٍ، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ شُعَيْبٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ، قَالَ: لَيْسَ عَلَيْهِ قَطْعٌ حَتَّى يَخْرُجَ بِالْمَتَاعِ مِنَ الْبَيْتِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Waki' -> Ibn Jurayj -> 'Amr ibn Shu'ayb -> Ibn 'Umar
english.text: Ibn Umar said: "There is no cutting [of the hand] until he takes the goods out of the house."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا حُمَيْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ، عَنْ مُوسَى بْنِ أَبِي الْفُرَاتِ، عَنْ عُمَرَ بْنِ عَبْدِ الْعَزِيزِ، قَالَ: لَا يُقْطَعُ حَتَّى يَخْرُجَ بِالْمَتَاعِ مِنَ الْبَيْتِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Humayd ibn 'Abd al-Rahman -> Musa ibn Abi al-Furat -> 'Umar ibn 'Abd al-'Aziz
english.text: Umar ibn Abd al-Aziz said: "[The hand] is not cut off until he takes the goods out of the house."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا حَفْصٌ، عَنْ حَجَّاجٍ، عَنْ حُصَيْنٍ الْحَارِثِيِّ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ، عَنِ الْحَارِثِ، عَنْ عَلِيٍّ، قَالَ: أُتِيَ بِرَجُلٍ قَدْ نَقَبَ، فَأُخِذَ عَلَى تِلْكَ الْحَالِ، فَلَمْ يَقْطَعْهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Hafs -> Hajjaj -> Husayn al-Harithi -> Al-Sha'bi -> Al-Harith -> Ali
english.text: Ali was brought a man who had dug a hole [to steal] and was caught in that state, but he did not cut off his hand.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو مُعَاوِيَةَ، عَنْ عَاصِمٍ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ، أَنَّهُ سُئِلَ عَنْ رَجُلٍ سَرَقَ سَرِقَةً، ثُمَّ كَوَّرَهَا، فَأُدْرِكَ قَبْلَ أَنْ يَخْرُجَ مِنَ الْبَيْتِ، قَالَ: لَيْسَ عَلَيْهِ قَطْعٌ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Abu Mu'awiyah -> 'Asim -> Al-Sha'bi
english.text: Al-Sha'bi was asked about a man who stole something and bundled it up but was caught before leaving the house. He said: "There is no cutting [of the hand] for him."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ مُسْهِرٍ، عَنْ زَكَرِيَّا، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ، قَالَ: لَيْسَ عَلَيْهِ قَطْعٌ حَتَّى يُخْرِجَ الْمَتَاعَ مِنَ الْبَيْتِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Ibn Mushir -> Zakariya -> Al-Sha'bi
english.text: Al-Sha'bi said: "There is no cutting [of the hand] for him until he takes the goods out of the house."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ بَكْرٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ جُرَيْجٍ، قَالَ: قُلْتُ لِعَطَاءٍ: يُؤْخَذُ السَّارِقُ قَدْ أَخَذَ الْمَتَاعَ وَقَدْ جَمَعَهُ فِي الْبَيْتِ؟، قَالَ: لَا قَطْعَ عَلَيْهِ حَتَّى يَخْرُجَ بِهِ زَعَمُوا، قَالَ: وَقَالَ لِي عَمْرُو بْنُ دِينَارٍ: مَا أَرَى عَلَيْهِ قَطْعًا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Muhammad ibn Bakr -> Ibn Jurayj -> 'Ata'
english.text: Ibn Jurayj said: I asked Ata: "A thief is caught having taken the goods and gathered them in the house?" He said: "There is no cutting [of the hand] for him until he takes it out, they claimed." 'Amr ibn Dinar also said to me: "I do not see any cutting for him."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْأَعْلَى، عَنْ دَاوُدَ، عَنْ أَبِي حَرْبِ بْنِ أَبِي الْأَسْوَدِ: أَنَّ لِصًّا نَقَبَ بَيْتَ قَوْمٍ، فَأَدْرَكَهُ الْحُرَّاسُ فَأَخَذُوهُ، فَرُفِعَ إِلَى أَبِي الْأَسْوَدِ، قَالَ: وَجَدْتُمْ مَعَهُ شَيْئًا، فَقَالُوا: لَا، قَالَ لِلنَّاسِ: أَرَادَ أَنْ يَسْرِقَ فَأَعْجَلْتُمُوهُ فَجَلَدَهُ خَمْسَةً وَعِشْرِينَ سَوْطًا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> 'Abd al-A'la -> Dawud -> Abu Harb ibn Abi al-Aswad
english.text: Abu Harb ibn Abi al-Aswad reported that a thief dug a hole into a people's house, but the guards caught him and took him. He was brought to Abu al-Aswad, who asked: "Did you find anything with him?" They said: "No." He said to the people: "He wanted to steal, but you hurried him." So he flogged him twenty-five lashes.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا يَزِيدُ بْنُ هَارُونَ، عَنْ حَمَّادِ بْنِ سَلَمَةَ، عَنْ حُمَيْدٍ: أَنَّ عُمَرَ بْنَ عَبْدِ الْعَزِيزِ كَتَبَ فِي سَارِقٍ: لَا يُقْطَعُ حَتَّى يَخْرُجَ بِالْمَتَاعِ مِنَ الدَّارِ، لَعَلَّهُ يَعْرِضُ تَوْبَةً قَبْلَ أَنْ يَخْرُجَ مِنَ الدَّارِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Yazid ibn Harun -> Hammad ibn Salamah -> Humayd
english.text: Umar ibn Abd al-Aziz wrote regarding a thief: "He is not cut until he takes the goods out of the house; perhaps he might repent before leaving the house."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو خَالِدٍ الْأَحْمَرُ، عَنْ يَحْيَى بْنِ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ الْقَاسِمِ، قَالَ: بَلَغَ عَائِشَةَ أَنَّهُمْ يَقُولُونَ: إِذَا لَمْ يَخْرُجْ بِالْمَتَاعِ لَمْ يُقْطَعْ، فَقَالَتْ: لَوْ لَمْ أَجِدْ إِلَّا سِكِّينًا لَقَطَعْتُهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Abu Khalid al-Ahmar -> Yahya ibn Sa'id -> 'Abd al-Rahman ibn al-Qasim
english.text: Abd al-Rahman ibn al-Qasim said: It reached Aisha that they were saying: "If he does not take the goods out, he is not cut." She said: "If I found nothing but a knife [on him], I would cut him."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا جَرِيرٌ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: إِذَا زَنَى وَسَرَقَ وَقَتَلَ وَعَمِلَ حُدُودًا، قَالَ: يُقْتَلُ، وَلَا يُزَادُ عَلَى ذَلِكَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Jarir -> Mughirah -> Ibrahim
