arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مُعَاذُ بْنُ مُعَاذٍ، عَنْ أَشْعَثَ، فِي الرَّجُلِ يَقُولُ لِلرَّجُلِ: يَا شَارِبَ خَمْرٍ يَا سَكْرَانُ، قَالَ: كَانَ لَا يَرَى عَلَيْهِ حَدًّا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Mu'adh b. Mu'adh -> Ash'ath
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Mu'adh ibn Mu'adh narrated to us, from Ash'ath, regarding a man who says to another: "O wine drinker, O drunkard," he said: "He did not see any hadd punishment upon him."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا الضَّحَّاكُ بْنُ مَخْلَدٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ جُرَيْجٍ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، فِي الرَّجُلِ يَقُولُ لِلرَّجُلِ: يَا شَارِبَ خَمْرٍ، يَا سَارِقُ، قَالَ: لَيْسَ عَلَيْهِ حَدٌّ، وَلَكِنْ سِيَاطٌ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Al-Dahhak b. Makhlad -> Ibn Jurayj -> 'Ata'
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Al-Dahhak ibn Makhlad narrated to us, from Ibn Jurayj, from 'Ata', regarding a man who says to another: "O wine drinker, O thief," he said: "There is no hadd punishment upon him, but lashes (Ta'zir)."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا الضَّحَّاكُ بْنُ مَخْلَدٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ جُرَيْجٍ، عَنْ سُلَيْمَانَ بْنِ مُوسَى، قَالَ: سَأَلْنَا عُمَرَ بْنَ عَبْدِ الْعَزِيزِ عَنْ رَجُلٍ قَالَ لِرَجُلٍ: يَا شَارِبَ خَمْرٍ، وَيَا مُشْرِكُ، وَيَا سَكْرَانُ، قُلْنَا: يُحَدُّ، قَالَ: سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ، مَا يُحَدُّ إِلَّا مَنْ قَذَفَ مُسْلِمًا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Al-Dahhak b. Makhlad -> Ibn Jurayj -> Sulayman b. Musa
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Al-Dahhak ibn Makhlad narrated to us, from Ibn Jurayj, from Sulayman ibn Musa, who said: We asked 'Umar ibn 'Abd al-'Aziz about a man who said to another: "O wine drinker, O polytheist, O drunkard." We said: "He should be punished (hadd)." He said: "Glory be to Allah! Only the one who slanders a Muslim (with adultery) receives the hadd punishment."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ يَمَانٍ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ جَابِرٍ، عَنْ عَامِرٍ، فِي الرَّجُلِ يَقُولُ لِلرَّجُلِ: يَا شَارِبَ خَمْرٍ، قَالَ: لَا يُضْرَبُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Ibn Yaman -> Sufyan -> Jabir -> 'Amir
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ibn Yaman narrated to us, from Sufyan, from Jabir, from 'Amir, regarding a man who says to another: "O wine drinker," he said: "He is not beaten."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا جَرِيرٌ، عَنْ مَنْصُورٍ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، فِي رَجُلٍ لَاعَنَ امْرَأَتَهُ فَفُرِّقَ بَيْنَهُمَا، ثُمَّ أَكْذَبَ نَفْسَهُ، قَالَ: يُجْلَدُ وَيُلْزَقُ بِهِ الْوَلَدُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Jarir -> Mansur -> Ibrahim
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Jarir narrated to us, from Mansur, from Ibrahim, regarding a man who invoked curses (li'an) against his wife and they were separated, then he belied himself. He said: "He is whipped, and the child is attributed to him."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا حَفْصٌ، عَنْ دَاوُدَ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ الْمُسَيِّبِ، فِي الْمُلَاعَنِ يُكَذِّبُ نَفْسَهُ، قَالَ: يُضْرَبُ وَهُوَ خَاطِبٌ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Hafs -> Dawud -> Sa'id b. al-Musayyib
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Hafs narrated to us, from Dawud, from Sa'id ibn al-Musayyib, regarding the one who invokes curses (li'an) and belies himself. He said: "He is beaten, and he is a suitor (can remarry her)."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرِ بْنُ عَيَّاشٍ، عَنْ مُطَرِّفٍ، قَالَ: إِذَا قَذَفَ الرَّجُلُ امْرَأَتَهُ لَاعَنَهَا، فَإِنْ أَكْذَبَ نَفْسَهُ بَعْدَ ذَلِكَ جُلِدَ، وَيُلْزَقُ بِهِ الْوَلَدُ، وَرُدَّتْ إِلَيْهِ امْرَأَتُهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Abu Bakr b. Ayyash -> Mutarrif
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Abu Bakr ibn 'Ayyash narrated to us, from Mutarrif, who said: "If a man slanders his wife, he invokes curses (li'an) against her. If he belies himself after that, he is whipped, the child is attributed to him, and his wife is returned to him."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، فِي الْمَلَاعِنِ يُكَذِّبُ نَفْسَهُ، قَالَ: يُجْلَدُ الْحَدَّ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Mughirah -> Ibrahim
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: From Mughirah, from Ibrahim, regarding the one who invokes curses (li'an) and belies himself. He said: "He is whipped the hadd punishment."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا غُنْدَرٌ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، عَنِ الْحَكَمِ، قَالَ: سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ الرَّجُلِ يُلَاعِنُ امْرَأَتَهُ ثُمَّ أَقَرَّ بِالْوَلَدِ، قَالَ: يُضْرَبُ الْحَدَّ وَيَلْزَقُ بِهِ الْوَلَدُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Ghundar -> Shu'bah -> Al-Hakam
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ghundar narrated to us, from Shu'bah, from Al-Hakam, who said: I asked him about a man who invokes curses (li'an) against his wife and then acknowledges the child. He said: "He is whipped the hadd punishment, and the child is attributed to him."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو عَاصِمٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ جُرَيْجٍ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، فِي الرَّجُلِ يَقْذِفُ امْرَأَتَهُ، أَوْ يَنْتَفِي مِنْ وَلَدِ امْرَأَتِهِ ثُمَّ يُكَذِّبُ نَفْسَهُ، قَالَ: يُحَدُّ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Abu Asim -> Ibn Jurayj -> 'Ata'
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Abu 'Asim narrated to us, from Ibn Jurayj, from 'Ata', regarding a man who slanders his wife or denies his wife's child, then belies himself. He said: "He is given the hadd punishment."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنِ ابْنِ شُبْرُمَةَ، عَنِ الْحَارِثِ، وعَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنِ ابْنِ جُرَيْجٍ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، وعَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ جَابِرٍ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ، فِي الْمَلَاعِنِ يُكَذِّبُ نَفْسَهُ، قَالُوا: يُضْرَبُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Waki' -> Sufyan -> Ibn Shubrumah -> Al-Harith / Ibn Jurayj -> 'Ata' / Jabir -> Al-Sha'bi
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Waki' narrated to us, from Sufyan, from Ibn Shubrumah, from Al-Harith, and from Sufyan, from Ibn Jurayj, from 'Ata', and from Sufyan, from Jabir, from Al-Sha'bi, regarding the one who invokes curses (li'an) and belies himself. They said: "He is beaten."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مُعْتَمِرُ بْنُ سُلَيْمَانَ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ الزُّبَيْرِ، عَنْ مَكْحُولٍ، قَالَ: إِذَا لَاعَنَ الرَّجُلُ، وَأَبَتِ الْمَرْأَةُ أَنْ تُلَاعِنَ رُجِمَتْ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Mu'tamir b. Sulayman -> Muhammad b. al-Zubayr -> Makhul
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Mu'tamir ibn Sulayman narrated to us, from Muhammad ibn al-Zubayr, from Makhul, who said: "If a man invokes curses (li'an) and the woman refuses to invoke curses, she is stoned."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مُعَاذُ بْنُ مُعَاذٍ، عَنْ أَشْعَثَ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، قَالَ: تُحْبَسُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Mu'adh b. Mu'adh -> Ash'ath -> Al-Hasan
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Mu'adh ibn Mu'adh narrated to us, from Ash'ath, from Al-Hasan, who said: "She is imprisoned."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ الْمُحَارِبِيُّ، عَنْ جُوَيْبِرٍ، عَنِ الضَّحَّاكِ، فِي الرَّجُلِ يَقْذِفُ امْرَأَتَهُ، فَتَأْبَى أَنْ تُلَاعِنَهُ، قَالَ: تُجْلَدُ مِائَةً وَتُرْجَمُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Abd al-Rahman al-Muharibi -> Juwaybir -> Al-Dahhak
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: 'Abd al-Rahman al-Muharibi narrated to us, from Juwaybir, from Al-Dahhak, regarding a man who slanders his wife, and she refuses to invoke curses against him. He said: "She is lashed a hundred times and stoned."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مُعْتَمِرٌ، عَنْ عِيسَى الْخَيَّاطِ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ، قَالَ: مَنْ وَقَعَ عَلَيْهِ اللِّعَانُ فَأَبَى أَنْ يَحْلِفَ أُقِيمَ عَلَيْهِ الْحَدُّ وَقَالَ عِيسَى: سَمِعْتُ عِنْدَ الشَّعْبِيِّ، يَقُولُ: يُجْبَرَانِ عَلَى اللِّعَانِ وَيُحْبَسَانِ حَتَّى يَتَلَاعَنَا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Mu'tamir -> Isa al-Khayyat -> Al-Sha'bi
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Mu'tamir narrated to us, from 'Isa al-Khayyat, from Al-Sha'bi, who said: "Whoever is subject to li'an and refuses to swear, the hadd punishment is carried out on him." 'Isa said: I heard Al-Sha'bi say: "They are compelled to li'an and imprisoned until they invoke curses against each other."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا يَحْيَى بْنُ يَمَانٍ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ مُطَرِّفٍ، وجَابِرٍ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ، وعَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ لَيْثٍ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ، وَعَطَاءٍ، قَالُوا: إِذَا دُرِئَ فِي اللِّعَانِ أُلْزِقَ بِهِ الْوَلَدُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Yahya b. Yaman -> Sufyan -> Mutarrif / Jabir -> Al-Sha'bi / Layth -> Mujahid / 'Ata'
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Yahya ibn Yaman narrated to us, from Sufyan, from Mutarrif and Jabir, from Al-Sha'bi, and from Sufyan, from Layth, from Mujahid and 'Ata', who said: "If the li'an is averted, the child is attributed to him."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ السَّلَامِ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، فِي الرَّجُلِ يُلَاعِنُ امْرَأَتَهُ، ثُمَّ يَقْذِفُهَا، قَالَ: يُضْرَبُ، وَقَالَ عَامِرٌ: لَا يُضْرَبُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Abd al-Salam -> Mughirah -> Ibrahim
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: 'Abd al-Salam narrated to us, from Mughirah, from Ibrahim, regarding a man who invokes curses (li'an) against his wife and then slanders her. He said: "He is beaten." 'Amir said: "He is not beaten."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا شَبَابَةُ بْنُ سَوَّارٍ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ الْحَكَمَ، وَحَمَّادًا عَنِ الرَّجُلِ يُلَاعِنُ امْرَأَتَهُ ثُمَّ تَلِدُ، فَيَقُولُ: لَيْسَ هَذَا مِنِّي، قَالَا: يُضْرَبُ وَقَدْ أَكْذَبَ نَفْسَهُ، قَالَ: لَا يُحَدُّ، قَدْ بَاءَ بِلَعْنَةِ اللَّهِ فِي كِتَابِ اللَّهِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Shababah b. Sawwar -> Shu'bah -> Al-Hakam / Hammad
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Shababah ibn Sawwar narrated to us, from Shu'bah, who said: I asked Al-Hakam and Hammad about a man who invokes curses (li'an) against his wife, then she gives birth, and he says: "This is not from me." They said: "He is beaten." And he had belied himself. He said: "He is not given the hadd punishment; he has incurred the curse of Allah in the Book of Allah."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا حَفْصٌ، عَنْ أَشْعَثَ، عَنِ الْحَكَمِ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: "إِذَا قَذَفَ الْمَجْلُودُ امْرَأَتَهُ جُلِدَ، وَلَا لِعَانَ بَيْنَهُمَا، قَالَ: وَسَأَلْتُ الْحَسَنَ وَعَامِرًا فَقَالَا: يُلَاعِنُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Hafs -> Ash'ath -> Al-Hakam -> Ibrahim
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Hafs narrated to us, from Ash'ath, from Al-Hakam, from Ibrahim, who said: "If a flogged person slanders his wife, he is flogged, and there is no li'an between them." He said: I asked Al-Hasan and 'Amir, and they said: "He invokes li'an."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو خَالِدٍ الْأَحْمَرُ، عَنْ أَشْعَثَ، عَنْ مَنْصُورٍ، وحَمَّادٍ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: إِذَا قَذَفَ الرَّجُلُ امْرَأَتَهُ وَقَدْ كَانَ جُلِدَ الْحَدَّ جُلِدَ، وَلَا يُلَاعِنُ لِأَنَّهَا لَا تَجُوزُ شَهَادَتُهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Abu Khalid al-Ahmar -> Ash'ath -> Mansur / Hammad -> Ibrahim
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Abu Khalid al-Ahmar narrated to us, from Ash'ath, from Mansur and Hammad, from Ibrahim, who said: "If a man slanders his wife and he had previously been flogged the hadd punishment, he is flogged, and he does not invoke li'an because his testimony is not accepted."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبَّادُ بْنُ الْعَوَّامِ، عَنْ عُمَرَ بْنِ عَامِرٍ، عَنْ حَمَّادٍ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: إِذَا أَكْذَبَ الرَّجُلُ نَفْسَهُ قَبْلَ مَا بَقِيَ مِنْ مُلَاعَنَتِهَا شَيْءٌ جُلِدَ وَهِيَ امْرَأَتُهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Abbad b. al-Awwam -> Umar b. Amir -> Hammad -> Ibrahim
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: 'Abbad ibn al-'Awwam narrated to us, from 'Umar ibn 'Amir, from Hammad, from Ibrahim, who said: "If a man belies himself before anything remains of his li'an against her, he is flogged, and she remains his wife."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبَّادٌ، عَنْ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ أَبِي مَعْشَرٍ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، مِثْلَهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Abbad -> Sa'id -> Abu Ma'shar -> Ibrahim
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: 'Abbad narrated to us, from Sa'id, from Abu Ma'shar, from Ibrahim, similar to it.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو أُسَامَةَ، عَنْ هِشَامٍ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، قَالَ: إِذَا أَكْذَبَ نَفْسَهُ قَبْلَ أَنْ تَنْقَضِيَ الْمُلَاعَنَةُ جُلِدَ وَهِيَ امْرَأَتُهُ، وَإِنْ أَكْذَبَ نَفْسَهُ بَعْدَ الْمُلَاعَنَةِ فَلَا شَيْءَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Abu Usamah -> Hisham -> Al-Hasan
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Abu Usamah narrated to us, from Hisham, from Al-Hasan, who said: "If he belies himself before the li'an is completed, he is flogged and she remains his wife. But if he belies himself after the li'an, there is nothing."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا جَرِيرٌ، عَنْ بَيَانٍ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ، قَالَ: مَنْ قَذَفَ ابْنَ الْمُلَاعَنَةِ أَوْ قَذَفَ أُمَّهُ ضُرِبَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Jarir -> Bayan -> Al-Sha'bi
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Jarir narrated to us, from Bayan, from Al-Sha'bi, who said: "Whoever slanders the son of the woman involved in li'an or slanders his mother is beaten."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا شَرِيكٌ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، وعَنْ لَيْثٍ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ، وطَاوُسٍ فِي الرَّجُلِ يَقُولُ لِابْنِ الْمُلَاعَنَةِ يَا ابْنَ الزَّانِيَةِ أَوْ قَذَفَ أُمَّهُ: ضُرِبَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Sharik -> Mughirah -> Ibrahim / Layth -> Mujahid / Tawus
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Sharik narrated to us, from Mughirah, from Ibrahim, and from Layth, from Mujahid and Tawus, regarding a man who says to the son of a woman involved in li'an: "O son of an adulteress," or slanders his mother. They said: "He is beaten."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا شَرِيكٌ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، وعَنْ لَيْثٍ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ، وعَنْ جَابِرٍ، وَابْنِ سَالِمٍ، عَنْ عَامِرٍ قَالُوا: مَنْ قَذَفَ ابْنَ الْمُلَاعَنَةِ جُلِدَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Sharik -> Mughirah -> Ibrahim / Layth -> Mujahid / Jabir / Ibn Salim -> 'Amir
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Sharik narrated to us, from Mughirah, from Ibrahim, and from Layth, from Mujahid, and from Jabir and Ibn Salim, from 'Amir, who all said: "Whoever slanders the son of a woman involved in li'an is flogged."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ السَّلَامِ، عَنْ لَيْثٍ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ، وطَاوُسٍ، فِي الرَّجُلِ يَقُولُ لِابْنِ الْمُلَاعَنَةِ: يَا ابْنَ الزَّانِيَةِ، قَالَا: يُجْلَدُ ثَمَانِينَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Abd al-Salam -> Layth -> Mujahid / Tawus
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: 'Abd al-Salam narrated to us, from Layth, from Mujahid and Tawus, regarding a man who says to the son of a woman involved in li'an: "O son of an adulteress." They said: "He is flogged eighty lashes."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرِ بْنُ عَيَّاشٍ، عَنْ مُوسَى بْنِ عُبَيْدَةَ، عَنْ نَافِعٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ، قَالَ: مَنْ قَذَفَ ابْنَ الْمُلَاعَنَةِ جُلِدَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Abu Bakr b. Ayyash -> Musa b. Ubaydah -> Nafi' -> Ibn Umar
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Abu Bakr ibn 'Ayyash narrated to us, from Musa ibn 'Ubaydah, from Nafi', from Ibn 'Umar, who said: "Whoever slanders the son of a woman involved in li'an is flogged."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مُعَاذُ بْنُ مُعَاذٍ، عَنْ عِمْرَانَ، عَنْ عِكْرِمَةَ، قَالَ: "مَنْ قَالَ لِابْنِ الْمُلَاعَنَةِ: يَا ابْنَ الْهَنَةِ جُلِدَ الْحَدَّ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Mu'adh b. Mu'adh -> Imran -> Ikrimah
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Mu'adh ibn Mu'adh narrated to us, from 'Imran, from 'Ikrimah, who said: "Whoever says to the son of a woman involved in li'an: 'O son of the lowly one,' is flogged the hadd punishment."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَسْبَاطُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ، عَنْ مُطَرِّفٍ، عَنْ عَامِرٍ، قَالَ: إِذَا قِيلَ لِابْنِ الْمُلَاعَنَةِ: لَسْتَ بِابْنِ فُلَانٍ الَّذِي لَاعَنَ أُمَّكَ، قَالَ: يُجْلَدُ الَّذِي يَقُولُ لَهُ ذَلِكَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Asbat b. Muhammad -> Mutarrif -> 'Amir
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Asbat ibn Muhammad narrated to us, from Mutarrif, from 'Amir, who said: If it is said to the son of a woman involved in li'an: "You are not the son of so-and-so who invoked curses against your mother," he said: "The one who says that to him is flogged."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ عَبَّادِ بْنِ مَنْصُورٍ، عَنْ عِكْرِمَةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، قَالَ: مَنْ رَمَى ابْنَ الْمُلَاعَنَةِ أَوْ أُمَّهُ جُلِدَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Waki' -> 'Abbad b. Mansur -> Ikrimah -> Ibn Abbas
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Waki' narrated to us, from 'Abbad ibn Mansur, from 'Ikrimah, from Ibn 'Abbas, who said: "Whoever accuses the son of a woman involved in li'an or his mother is flogged."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنِ الْفَضْلِ بْنِ دَلْهَمٍ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، قَالَ: يُضْرَبُ قَاذِفُ ابْنِ الْمُلَاعَنَةِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Waki' -> Al-Fadl b. Dalham -> Al-Hasan
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Waki' narrated to us, from Al-Fadl ibn Dalham, from Al-Hasan, who said: "The slanderer of the son of a woman involved in li'an is beaten."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا جَرِيرٌ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: إِنْ قَذَفَهَا إِنْسَانٌ جُلِدَ قَاذِفُهَا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Jarir -> Mughirah -> Ibrahim
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Jarir narrated to us, from Mughirah, from Ibrahim, who said: "If a person slanders her, her slanderer is flogged."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا حَفْصٌ، عَنْ أَشْعَثَ، عَنِ الْحَكَمِ، وحَمَّادٍ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، فِي الْأَمَةِ تَكُونُ تَحْتَ الْحُرِّ فَيَقْذِفُهَا، قَالَ: لَا يَضْرِبُ الْحَدَّ، وَلَا يُلَاعِنُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Hafs -> Ash'ath -> Al-Hakam / Hammad -> Ibrahim
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Hafs narrated to us, from Ash'ath, from Al-Hakam and Hammad, from Ibrahim, regarding a slave woman married to a free man who slanders her. He said: "He does not strike the hadd punishment, nor does he invoke li'an."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ السَّلَامِ، عَنْ مُطَرِّفٍ، عَنْ عَامِرٍ، فِي الْأَمَةِ تَكُونُ تَحْتَ الْحُرِّ فَيَقْذِفُهَا، قَالَ: لَا حَدَّ عَلَيْهَا، وَلَا لِعَانَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Abd al-Salam -> Mutarrif -> 'Amir
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: 'Abd al-Salam narrated to us, from Mutarrif, from 'Amir, regarding a slave woman married to a free man who slanders her. He said: "There is no hadd punishment on her, nor li'an."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مُعْتَمِرٌ، عَنْ لَيْثٍ، عَنْ طَاوُسٍ، ومُجَاهِدٍ، وَالْحَكَمِ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، وَالشَّعْبِيِّ فِي الرَّجُلِ يَكُونُ تَحْتَهُ الْأَمَةُ فَيَقْذِفُهَا، قَالُوا: لَيْسَ بَيْنَهُمَا تَلَاعُنٌ، وَلَيْسَ عَلَى قَاذِفِهَا حَدٌّ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Mu'tamir -> Layth -> Tawus / Mujahid / Al-Hakam -> Ibrahim / Al-Sha'bi
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Mu'tamir narrated to us, from Layth, from Tawus, Mujahid, Al-Hakam, from Ibrahim and Al-Sha'bi, regarding a man who has a slave woman (as a wife) and slanders her. They said: "There is no li'an between them, and there is no hadd punishment on her slanderer."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا غُنْدَرٌ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، عَنِ الْحَكَمِ، وَحَمَّادٍ، فِي الْعَبْدِ تَكُونُ تَحْتَهُ الْحُرَّةُ فَيَقْذِفُهَا، قَالَا: لَيْسَ بَيْنَهُمَا مُلَاعَنَةٌ، وَيُجْلَدُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Ghundar -> Shu'bah -> Al-Hakam / Hammad
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ghundar narrated to us, from Shu'bah, from Al-Hakam and Hammad, regarding a slave who has a free woman (as a wife) and slanders her. They said: "There is no li'an between them, and he is flogged."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عُبَيْدٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الْمَلِكِ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، فِي الْيَهُودِيَّةِ تُلَاعِنُ الْمُسْلِمَ، قَالَ: لَا، وَلَا الْعَبْدُ الْحُرَّةَ، وَلَكِنْ يُجْلَدُ الْعَبْدُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Muhammad b. Ubayd -> Abd al-Malik -> 'Ata'
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Muhammad ibn 'Ubayd narrated to us, from 'Abd al-Malik, from 'Ata', regarding a Jewish woman engaging in li'an with a Muslim. He said: "No, nor a slave with a free woman, but the slave is flogged."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا حُمَيْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ، عَنْ حَسَنٍ، عَنْ مُطَرِّفٍ، عَنِ الْحَكَمِ، وعَامِرٍ، فِي الْمَمْلُوكِ تَكُونُ لَهُ امْرَأَةٌ حُرَّةٌ، فَتَجِيءُ بِوَلَدٍ فَيَنْتَفِي مِنْهُ، قَالَ: يُضْرَبُ وَلَا لِعَانَ بَيْنَهُمَا، وَيُلْزَقُ بِهِ الْوَلَدُ وَقَالَ عَامِرٌ وَالْحَكَمُ فِي الْحُرِّ تَحْتَهُ الْأَمَةُ، فَجَاءَتْ بِوَلَدٍ فَانْتَفَى مِنْهُ، قَالَ: لَيْسَ بَيْنَهُمَا لِعَانٌ، وَيُلْزَقُ بِهِ الْوَلَدُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Humayd b. Abd al-Rahman -> Hasan -> Mutarrif -> Al-Hakam / 'Amir
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Humayd ibn 'Abd al-Rahman narrated to us, from Hasan, from Mutarrif, from Al-Hakam and 'Amir, regarding a slave who has a free wife, and she brings a child, and he denies it. He said: "He is beaten, there is no li'an between them, and the child is attributed to him." 'Amir and Al-Hakam said regarding a free man who has a slave wife, and she brings a child, and he denies it: "There is no li'an between them, and the child is attributed to him."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْأَعْلَى، عَنْ مَعْمَرٍ، عَنِ الزُّهْرِيِّ، فِي الْعَبْدِ إِذَا كَانَ تَحْتَهُ الْحُرَّةُ أَنَّهُ: إِذَا قَذَفَهَا جُلِدَ، وَلَا يُلَاعِنُ، وَإِذَا كَانَ حُرٌّ تَحْتَهُ أَمَةٌ، فَقَذَفَهَا فَإِنَّهُ لَا يُجْلَدُ وَلَا يُلَاعِنُ، وَإِذَا كَانَ عَبْدٌ تَحْتَهُ أَمَةٌ فَقَذَفَهَا، فَإِنَّهُ يُحَدُّ، وَلَا يُلَاعِنُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Abd al-A'la -> Ma'mar -> Al-Zuhri
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: 'Abd al-A'la narrated to us, from Ma'mar, from Al-Zuhri, regarding a slave who has a free wife: "If he slanders her, he is flogged, and he does not invoke li'an. If a free man has a slave wife and slanders her, he is neither flogged nor invokes li'an. If a slave has a slave wife and slanders her, he is punished (hadd), and he does not invoke li'an."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا سَهْلُ بْنُ يُوسُفَ، عَنْ عَمْرٍو، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، فِي أَرْبَعَةٍ شَهِدُوا عَلَى رَجُلٍ أَنَّهُ طَلَّقَ امْرَأَتَهُ ثَلَاثًا، فَأَنْكَرَ وَأَقَرَّ بِغَشَيَانِ الْمَرْأَةِ، فَقَالَ: لَا حَدَّ عَلَيْهِ لِأَنَّهُ مُخَاصِمٌ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Sahl b. Yusuf -> Amr -> Al-Hasan
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Sahl ibn Yusuf narrated to us, from 'Amr, from Al-Hasan, regarding four witnesses who testified that a man divorced his wife three times, but he denied it and admitted to having intercourse with the woman. He said: "There is no hadd punishment on him because he is a disputant."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْأَعْلَى، عَنْ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، عَنْ جَابِرِ بْنِ زَيْدٍ، وَهُوَ قَوْلُ قَتَادَةَ إِنَّهُمَا قَالَا: يُفَرَّقُ بَيْنَهُمَا بِشَهَادَةِ اثْنَيْنِ وَثَلَاثَةٍ، وَيُرْجَمُ بِشَهَادَةِ أَرْبَعَةٍ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Abd al-A'la -> Sa'id -> Qatadah -> Jabir b. Zayd
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: 'Abd al-A'la narrated to us, from Sa'id, from Qatadah, from Jabir ibn Zayd, and it is the opinion of Qatadah, that they both said: "They are separated by the testimony of two or three, and he is stoned by the testimony of four."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْأَعْلَى، عَنْ سَعِيدٍ، قَالَ: نَبَّأُوا عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ أَبِي ذِئْبٍ، عَنْ عُمَرَ، قَالَ: يُفَرَّقُ بَيْنَهُمَا بِشَهَادَةِ اثْنَيْنِ أَوْ ثَلَاثَةٍ، وَيُرْجَمُ بِشَهَادَةِ أَرْبَعَةٍ وَأَكْثَرَ، فَإِنْ عَادَ رُجِمَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Abd al-A'la -> Sa'id -> Sa'id b. Abi Dhi'b -> Umar
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: 'Abd al-A'la narrated to us, from Sa'id, who said: They reported from Sa'id ibn Abi Dhi'b, from 'Umar, who said: "They are separated by the testimony of two or three, and he is stoned by the testimony of four or more. If he returns, he is stoned."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ سَالِمٍ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ، أَنَّهُ سُئِلَ عَنْ رَجُلٍ، شَهِدَ عَلَيْهِ شُهُودٌ أَنَّهُ طَلَّقَ امْرَأَتَهُ ثَلَاثًا، فَجَحَدَ ذَلِكَ وَإِنْ كَانَ يَغْشَاهَا، قَالَ: فَقَالَ: يُدْرَأُ عَنْهُ الْحَدُّ لِإِنْكَارِهِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Hushaym -> Muhammad b. Salim -> Al-Sha'bi
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Hushaym narrated to us, from Muhammad ibn Salim, from Al-Sha'bi, that he was asked about a man against whom witnesses testified that he divorced his wife three times, but he denied it although he was having intercourse with her. He said: "The hadd punishment is averted from him due to his denial."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا الضَّحَّاكُ بْنُ مَخْلَدٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ جُرَيْجٍ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، فِي رَجُلٍ طَلَّقَ امْرَأَتَهُ، فَأَشْهَدَ شَاهِدَيْنِ، ثُمَّ قَدِمَ الْقَرْيَةَ الَّتِي بِهَا الْمَرْأَةُ، فَغَشِيَهَا وَأَقَرَّ بِأَنْ قَدْ أَصَابَهَا، وَأَنْكَرَ أَنْ يَكُونَ قَدْ طَلَّقَهَا، فَقَالَ عَطَاءٌ: تَجُوزُ شَهَادَتُهُمَا، وَيُفَرَّقُ بَيْنَهُمَا، وَلَا يُحَدُّ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Al-Dahhak b. Makhlad -> Ibn Jurayj -> 'Ata'
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Al-Dahhak ibn Makhlad narrated to us, from Ibn Jurayj, from 'Ata', regarding a man who divorced his wife and made two witnesses testify, then came to the village where the woman was, had intercourse with her, admitted that he had intercourse with her, but denied that he had divorced her. 'Ata' said: "Their testimony is accepted, they are separated, and he is not punished."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدَةُ، عَنْ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ: أَنَّ رَجُلًا، طَلَّقَ امْرَأَتَهُ ثَلَاثًا، ثُمَّ جَعَلَ يَغْشَاهَا بَعْدَ ذَلِكَ، فَسُئِلَ عَنْ ذَلِكَ عَمَّارٌ، فَقَالَ عَمَّارٌ: لَئِنْ قَدَرْتُ عَلَى هَذَا لَأَرْجُمَنَّهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> 'Abdah -> Sa'id -> Qatadah
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: 'Abdah narrated to us, from Sa'id, from Qatadah: That a man divorced his wife three times, then began to have intercourse with her after that. 'Ammar was asked about that, and 'Ammar said: "If I catch this one, I will stone him."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ سَوَاءٍ، عَنْ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، عَنْ خِلَاسٍ، عَنْ عَمَّارٍ، بِنَحْوِهِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Muhammad b. Sawa' -> Sa'id -> Qatadah -> Khilas -> Ammar
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Muhammad ibn Sawa' narrated to us, from Sa'id, from Qatadah, from Khilas, from 'Ammar, with something similar.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا حُمَيْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ، عَنْ جَرِيرِ بْنِ حَازِمٍ، عَنْ عِيسَى بْنِ عَاصِمٍ، قَالَ: خَرَجَ قَوْمٌ فِي سَفَرٍ، فَمَرُّوا بِرَجُلٍ فَنَزَلُوا بِهِ، فَطَلَّقَ امْرَأَتَهُ ثَلَاثًا، فَمَضَى الْقَوْمُ فِي سَفَرِهِمْ، ثُمَّ عَادُوا فَوَجَدُوهُ مَعَهَا، فَقَدَّمُوهُ إِلَى شُرَيْحٍ، فَقَالُوا: إِنَّ هَذَا طَلَّقَ امْرَأَتَهُ ثَلَاثًا وَوَجَدْنَاهُ مَعَهَا، فَأَنْكَرَ، قَالَ: تَشْهَدُونَ أَنَّهُ زَانٍ، فَأَعَادُوا عَلَيْهِ، فَفَرَّقَ بَيْنَهُمَا، وَلَمْ يَحُدَّهُمَا، وَأَجَازَ شَهَادَتَهُمْ

english.narrator: Humayd b. Abd al-Rahman -> Jarir b. Hazim -> Isa b. Asim
english.text: Humayd ibn 'Abd al-Rahman narrated to us, from Jarir ibn Hazim, from 'Isa ibn 'Asim, who said: A group went out on a journey and passed by a man, so they stayed with him. He divorced his wife three times. The group continued on their journey, then returned and found him with her. They brought him before Shurayh and said: "This man divorced his wife three times, and we found him with her." He denied it. He (Shurayh) said: "Do you testify that he is an adulterer?" They repeated their statement against him. So he separated them, did not punish them, and accepted their testimony.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ الْأَنْصَارِيُّ، عَنْ أَشْعَثَ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، فِي الرَّجُلِ يَقُولُ لِلرَّجُلِ: أَخْبَرَنِي فُلَانٌ أَنَّكَ زَنَيْتَ، قَالَ: لَيْسَ عَلَيْهِ حَدٌّ، لِأَنَّهُ أَضَافَهُ إِلَى غَيْرِهِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Muhammad b. Abd Allah al-Ansari -> Ash'ath -> Al-Hasan
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Muhammad ibn 'Abd Allah al-Ansari narrated to us, from Ash'ath, from Al-Hasan, regarding a man who says to another: "So-and-so told me that you committed adultery." He said: "There is no hadd punishment on him because he attributed it to someone else."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، عَنْ بَعْضِ أَصْحَابِهِ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ: أَنَّ رَجُلًا قَالَ لِرَجُلٍ: زَعَمَ فُلَانٌ أَنَّكَ زَانٍ، قَالَ: إِنْ جَاءَ بِالْبَيِّنَةِ، وَإِلَّا ضُرِبَ الْحَدَّ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Hushaym -> Some of his companions -> Al-Sha'bi
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Hushaym narrated to us, from some of his companions, from Al-Sha'bi: That a man said to another: "So-and-so claims that you are an adulterer." He said: "If he brings proof [he is truthful], otherwise he is beaten the hadd punishment."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، عَنْ مَنْصُورٍ، عَنِ الْحَارِثِ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: قَالَ عُمَرُ بْنُ الْخَطَّابِ: لَئِنْ أُعَطِّلَ الْحُدُودَ بِالشُّبُهَاتِ أَحَبُّ إِلَيَّ مِنْ أَنْ أُقِيمَهَا بِالشُّبُهَاتِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Hushaym -> Mansur -> Al-Harith -> Ibrahim
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Hushaym narrated to us, from Mansur, from Al-Harith, from Ibrahim, who said: 'Umar ibn al-Khattab said: "Suspending the hadd punishments due to doubts is more beloved to me than carrying them out with doubts."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ السَّلَامِ، عَنْ إِسْحَاقَ بْنِ أَبِي فَرْوَةَ، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ شُعَيْبٍ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ: أَنَّ مُعَاذًا، وَعَبْدَ اللَّهِ بْنَ مَسْعُودٍ، وعُقْبَةَ بْنَ عَامِرٍ، قَالُوا: إِذَا اشْتَبَهَ عَلَيْكَ الْحَدُّ، فَادْرَأْهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Abd al-Salam -> Ishaq b. Abi Farwah -> Amr b. Shu'ayb -> His Father
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: 'Abd al-Salam narrated to us, from Ishaq ibn Abi Farwah, from 'Amr ibn Shu'ayb, from his father: That Mu'adh, 'Abd Allah ibn Mas'ud, and 'Uqbah ibn 'Amir said: "If the hadd punishment is doubtful to you, avert it."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ مَهْدِيٍّ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ قَيْسِ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ، عَنْ طَارِقِ بْنِ شِهَابٍ: أَنَّ امْرَأَةً زَنَتْ، فَقَالَ عُمَرُ: أَرَاهَا كَانَتْ تُصَلِّي مِنَ اللَّيْلِ فَخَشَعَتْ فَرَكَعَتْ فَسَجَدَتْ، فَأَتَاهَا غَاوٍ مِنَ الْغُوَاةِ فَتَحَتَّمَهَا، فَأَرْسَلَ عُمَرُ إِلَيْهَا فَقَالَتْ كَمَا، قَالَ عُمَرُ: فَخَلَّى سَبِيلَهَا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Ibn Mahdi -> Sufyan -> Qays b. Muslim -> Tariq b. Shihab
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ibn Mahdi narrated to us, from Sufyan, from Qays ibn Muslim, from Tariq ibn Shihab: That a woman committed adultery. 'Umar said: "I think she was praying at night, humbled herself, bowed, and prostrated, then a seducer came to her and overwhelmed her." 'Umar sent for her, and she said as 'Umar had said. So he let her go.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ فُضَيْلٍ، عَنِ الْأَعْمَشِ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: كَانُوا يَقُولُونَ: ادْرَءُوا الْحُدُودَ عَنْ عِبَادِ اللَّهِ، مَا اسْتَطَعْتُمْ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Ibn Fudayl -> Al-A'mash -> Ibrahim
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ibn Fudayl narrated to us, from Al-A'mash, from Ibrahim, who said: They used to say: "Avert the hadd punishments from the servants of Allah as much as you can."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْأَعْلَى، عَنْ بُرْدٍ، عَنِ الزُّهْرِيِّ، قَالَ: ادْفَعُوا الْحُدُودَ بِكُلِّ شُبْهَةٍ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Abd al-A'la -> Burd -> Al-Zuhri
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: 'Abd al-A'la narrated to us, from Burd, from Al-Zuhri, who said: "Push away the hadd punishments with every doubt."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ عَاصِمٍ، عَنْ أَبِي وَائِلٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ، قَالَ: ادْرَءُوا الْقَتْلَ وَالْجَلْدَ عَنِ الْمُسْلِمِينَ، مَا اسْتَطَعْتُمْ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Waki' -> Sufyan -> 'Asim -> Abu Wa'il -> 'Abd Allah
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Waki' narrated to us, from Sufyan, from 'Asim, from Abu Wa'il, from 'Abd Allah, who said: "Avert killing and flogging from the Muslims as much as you can."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا الْأَعْمَشُ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: قَالَ عُمَرُ: اطْرُدُوا الْمُعْتَرِفِينَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Waki' -> Al-A'mash -> Ibrahim
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Waki' narrated to us, saying: Al-A'mash narrated to us, from Ibrahim, who said: 'Umar said: "Dismiss those who confess."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ إِدْرِيسَ، عَنْ عَاصِمِ بْنِ كُلَيْبٍ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، قَالَ: قَالَ أَبُو مُوسَى: أُوتِيَتْ وَأَنَا بِالْيَمَنِ، امْرَأَةٌ حُبْلَى فَسَأَلْتُهَا، فَقَالَتْ: مَا تَسْأَلُ عَنِ امْرَأَةٍ حُبْلَى ثَيِّبٍ مِنْ غَيْرِ بَعْلٍ، أَمَا وَاللَّهِ مَا خَالَلْتُ خَلِيلًا وَلَا خَادَنْتُ خِدْنًا مُنْذُ أَسْلَمْتُ، وَلَكِنْ بَيْنَا أَنَا نَائِمَةٌ بِفِنَاءِ بَيْتِي وَاللَّهِ مَا أَيْقَظَنِي إِلَّا رَجُلٌ رَفَعَنِي وَأَلْقَى فِي بَطْنِي مِثْلَ الشِّهَابِ، ثُمَّ نَظَرْتُ إِلَيْهِ مُقَفًّى مَا أَدْرِي مَنْ هُوَ مِنْ خَلْقِ اللَّهِ، فَكَتَبْتُ فِيهَا إِلَى عُمَرَ فَكَتَبَ عُمَرُ: ائْتِنِي بِهَا وَبِنَاسٍ مِنْ قَوْمِهَا، قَالَ: فَوَافَيْنَاهُ بِالْمَوْسِمِ، فَقَالَ شَبَهَ الْغَضْبَانِ: لَعَلَّكَ قَدْ سَبَقْتَنِي بِشَيْءٍ مِنْ أَمْرِ امْرَأَةٍ؟، قَالَ: قُلْتُ: لَا، وَهِيَ مَعِي وَنَاسٌ مِنْ قَوْمِهَا فَسَأَلَهَا فَأَخْبَرَتْهُ كَمَا أَخْبَرَتْنِي، ثُمَّ سَأَلَ قَوْمَهَا فَأَثْنَوْا خَيْرًا، قَالَ:، فَقَالَ عُمَرُ: شَابَّةٌ تِهَامِيَّةٌ قَدْ نُوِّمَتْ، قَدْ كَانَ يَفْعَلُ قِمَارُهَا وَكَسَاهَا، وَأَوْصَى بِهَا قَوْمَهَا خَيْرًا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Ibn Idris -> Asim b. Kulayb -> His Father -> Abu Musa
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ibn Idris narrated to us, from 'Asim ibn Kulayb, from his father, who said: Abu Musa said: "While I was in Yemen, a pregnant woman was brought to me. I asked her, and she said: 'Why do you ask about a pregnant woman who has been previously married but has no husband? By Allah, I have not taken a lover nor a secret companion since I embraced Islam. But while I was sleeping in the courtyard of my house, by Allah, nothing woke me except a man lifting me and throwing something like a shooting star into my belly. Then I looked at him as he walked away, not knowing who he was among Allah's creation.' So I wrote about her to 'Umar. 'Umar wrote: 'Bring her to me along with some people from her tribe.' He said: So we met him during the season (Hajj). He said, seemingly angry: 'Perhaps you have preceded me in something regarding the woman's affair?' I said: 'No, she is with me along with people from her tribe.' So he asked her, and she told him what she had told me. Then he asked her people, and they spoke well of her. He said: So 'Umar said: 'A young Tihami woman who was put to sleep.' He would do things for her and clothed her, and advised her people to treat her well."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ إِدْرِيسَ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الْمَلِكِ بْنِ مَيْسَرَةَ، عَنِ النَّزَّالِ بْنِ سَبْرَةَ، قَالَ: بَيْنَمَا نَحْنُ بِمِنًى مَعَ عُمَرَ إِذَا امْرَأَةٌ ضَخْمَةٌ عَلَى حِمَارَةٍ تَبْكِي قَدْ كَادَ النَّاسُ أَنْ يَقْتُلُوهَا مِنَ الزِّحَامِ يَقُولُونَ: زَنَيَتْ. فَلَمَّا انْتَهَتْ إِلَى عُمَرَ، قَالَ: مَا يُبْكِيكَ؟ إِنَّ امْرَأَةً رُبَّمَا اسْتُكْرِهَتْ، فَقَالَتْ: كُنْتُ امْرَأَةً ثَقِيلَةَ الرَّأْسِ، وَكَانَ اللَّهُ يَرْزُقُنِي مِنْ صَلَاةِ اللَّيْلِ، فَصَلَّيْتُ لَيْلَةً ثُمَّ نِمْتُ، فَوَاللَّهِ مَا أَيْقَظَنِي إِلَّا الرَّجُلُ قَدْ رَكِبَنِي، فَرَأَيْتُ إِلَيْهِ مُقْفِيًا مَا أَدْرِي مَنْ هُوَ مِنْ خَلْقِ اللَّهِ، فَقَالَ عُمَرُ: لَوْ قُتِلَتْ هَذِهِ خَشِيتُ عَلَى الْأَخْشَبَيْنِ النَّارَ ثُمَّ كَتَبَ إِلَى الْأَمْصَارِ أَلَّا تُقْتَلَ نَفْسٌ دُونَهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Ibn Idris -> Shu'bah -> Abd al-Malik b. Maysarah -> Al-Nazzal b. Sabrah
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ibn Idris narrated to us, from Shu'bah, from 'Abd al-Malik ibn Maysarah, from Al-Nazzal ibn Sabrah, who said: "While we were at Mina with 'Umar, a large woman on a female donkey was crying, and the people were almost killing her from crowding, saying: 'She committed adultery.' When she reached 'Umar, he said: 'What makes you cry? A woman may be coerced.' She said: 'I was a heavy sleeper, and Allah blessed me with night prayer. I prayed one night and then slept. By Allah, nothing woke me except a man on top of me. I saw him leaving, not knowing who he was among Allah's creation.' 'Umar said: 'If she had been killed, I would have feared fire for the two mountains (of Mecca).' Then he wrote to the regions that no soul should be killed without his permission."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ يَزِيدَ بْنِ زِيَادٍ الْبَصْرِيِّ، عَنِ الزُّهْرِيِّ، عَنْ عُرْوَةَ، عَنْ عَائِشَةَ، قَالَتْ: ادْرَءُوا الْحُدُودَ عَنِ الْمُسْلِمِينَ، مَا اسْتَطَعْتُمْ، فَإِذَا وَجَدْتُمْ لِلْمُسْلِمِ مَخْرَجًا فَخَلُّوا سَبِيلَهُ، فَإِنَّ الْإِمَامَ إِذَا أَخْطَأَ فِي الْعَفْوِ خَيْرٌ مِنْ أَنْ يُخْطِئَ فِي الْعُقُوبَةِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Waki' -> Yazid b. Ziyad al-Basri -> Al-Zuhri -> Urwah -> A'ishah
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Waki' narrated to us, from Yazid ibn Ziyad al-Basri, from Al-Zuhri, from 'Urwah, from 'A'ishah, who said: "Avert the hadd punishments from the Muslims as much as you can. If you find a way out for a Muslim, let him go. For the Imam to err in pardon is better than for him to err in punishment."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرِ بْنُ عَيَّاشٍ، وَأَبُو الْأَحْوَصِ، عَنْ عَاصِمٍ، عَنْ أَبِي رَزِينٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، قَالَ: مَنْ أَتَى بَهِيمَةً فَلَا حَدَّ عَلَيْهِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Abu Bakr b. Ayyash / Abu al-Ahwas -> Asim -> Abu Razin -> Ibn Abbas
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Abu Bakr ibn 'Ayyash and Abu al-Ahwas narrated to us, from 'Asim, from Abu Razin, from Ibn 'Abbas, who said: "Whoever has intercourse with an animal, there is no hadd punishment upon him."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا جَرِيرٌ، عَنْ مَنْصُورٍ، عَنِ الْحَكَمِ، فِيمَنْ أَتَى بَهِيمَةً، قَالَ: يُجْلَدُ وَلَا يَبْلُغُ بِهِ الْحَدَّ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Jarir -> Mansur -> Al-Hakam
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Jarir narrated to us, from Mansur, from Al-Hakam, regarding someone who has intercourse with an animal. He said: "He is flogged, but not up to the hadd punishment."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبَّادُ بْنُ الْعَوَّامِ، عَنْ حَجَّاجٍ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، فِي الَّذِي يَأْتِي الْبَهِيمَةَ، قَالَ: يُعَزَّرُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Abbad b. al-Awwam -> Hajjaj -> 'Ata'
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: 'Abbad ibn al-'Awwam narrated to us, from Hajjaj, from 'Ata', regarding the one who has intercourse with an animal. He said: "He is chastised (Ta'zir)."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ جَابِرٍ، عَنْ عَامِرٍ، قَالَ: لَيْسَ عَلَى مَنْ أَتَى بَهِيمَةً حَدٌّ، وَلَا عَلَى مَنْ رَمَى بِهَا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Waki' -> Sufyan -> Jabir -> 'Amir
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Waki' narrated to us, from Sufyan, from Jabir, from 'Amir, who said: "There is no hadd punishment on the one who has intercourse with an animal, nor on the one who accuses someone of it."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عِيسَى بْنُ يُونُسَ، عَنْ أَبِي حَنِيفَةَ، عَنْ حَمَّادٍ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ:، قَالَ عُمَرُ: لَيْسَ عَلَى مَنْ أَتَى بَهِيمَةً حَدٌّ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Isa b. Yunus -> Abu Hanifah -> Hammad -> Ibrahim
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: 'Isa ibn Yunus narrated to us, from Abu Hanifah, from Hammad, from Ibrahim, who said: 'Umar said: "There is no hadd punishment on the one who has intercourse with an animal."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا حَفْصٌ، عَنْ عَبِيدَةَ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: مَنْ أَتَى بَهِيمَةً فَلَا حَدَّ عَلَيْهِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Hafs -> Ubaydah -> Ibrahim
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Hafs narrated to us, from 'Ubaydah, from Ibrahim, who said: "Whoever has intercourse with an animal, there is no hadd punishment on him."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْأَعْلَى، عَنْ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ بُدَيْلٍ، عَنْ جَابِرِ بْنِ زَيْدٍ، أَنَّهُ، قَالَ: إِذَا أَتَى الرَّجُلُ الْبَهِيمَةَ أُقِيمَ عَلَيْهِ الْحَدُّ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Abd al-A'la -> Sa'id -> Budayl -> Jabir b. Zayd
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: 'Abd al-A'la narrated to us, from Sa'id, from Budayl, from Jabir ibn Zayd, that he said: "If a man has intercourse with an animal, the hadd punishment is carried out on him."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا يَزِيدُ بْنُ هَارُونَ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ بْنُ حُسَيْنٍ، عَنْ أَبِي عَلِيٍّ الرَّحَبِيِّ، عَنْ عِكْرِمَةَ، قَالَ: سُئِلَ الْحَسَنُ بْنُ عَلِيٍّ عَنْ رَجُلٍ أَتَى بَهِيمَةً، قَالَ: إِنْ كَانَ مُحْصَنًا رُجِمَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Yazid b. Harun -> Sufyan b. Husayn -> Abu Ali al-Rahabi -> Ikrimah
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Yazid ibn Harun narrated to us, saying: Sufyan ibn Husayn narrated to us, from Abu 'Ali al-Rahabi, from 'Ikrimah, who said: Al-Hasan ibn 'Ali was asked about a man who had intercourse with an animal. He said: "If he is muhsan (married), he is stoned."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ السَّلَامِ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي فَرْوَةَ، عَنْ بُكَيْرِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ الْأَشَجِّ، عَنْ سُلَيْمَانَ بْنِ يَسَارٍ: أَنَّهُ كَانَ يُقِيمُ عَلَيْهِ الْحَدَّ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> 'Abd al-Salam -> Ibn Abi Farwah -> Bukayr ibn 'Abd Allah ibn al-Ashajj -> Sulayman ibn Yasar
english.text: Sulayman ibn Yasar used to carry out the Hadd punishment on him (i.e., someone who committed bestiality).

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا يَزِيدُ بْنُ هَارُونَ، عَنْ هِشَامٍ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، فِيمَنْ يَأْتِي الْبَهِيمَةَ وَالْغُلَامَ، قَالَ: عَلَيْهِ الْحَدُّ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Yazid ibn Harun -> Hisham -> Al-Hasan
english.text: Al-Hasan said regarding one who commits bestiality or sodomy: "The Hadd punishment is upon him."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مَعْنُ بْنُ عِيسَى، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي ذِئْبٍ، عَنِ الزُّهْرِيِّ، قَالَ: إِذَا وَقَعَ الرَّجُلُ بِالْبَهِيمَةِ جُلِدَ الْحَدَّ تَامًّا، وَمَنْ رَمَى امْرَأً بِالْبَهِيمَةِ فَعَلَيْهِ الْحَدُّ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Ma'n ibn 'Isa -> Ibn Abi Dhi'b -> Al-Zuhri
english.text: Al-Zuhri said: "If a man commits bestiality, he is flogged the full Hadd punishment. And whoever accuses a man of bestiality, the Hadd punishment is upon him."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ السَّلَامِ، عَنْ لَيْثٍ، عَنْ يَزِيدَ، عَنْ مَسْرُوقٍ، فِي الَّذِي يَأْتِي الْبَهِيمَةَ، قَالَ: "إِذَا فَعَلَ بِهَا، قَالَ: ذُبِحَتْ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> 'Abd al-Salam -> Layth -> Yazid -> Masruq
english.text: Masruq said regarding the one who commits bestiality: "If he does it with her [the animal], she is slaughtered."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ أَبِي عَدِيٍّ، عَنْ دَاوُدَ، قَالَ: قَالَ مَسْرُوقٌ: يُرْجَمُ وَتُرْجَمُ بِالْحِجَارَةِ الَّتِي رُجِمَ بِهَا، وَيُعْفَى أَثَرُهُ يَعْنِي فِي الَّذِي يَأْتِي الْبَهِيمَةَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Ibn Abi 'Adi -> Dawud -> Masruq
english.text: Masruq said regarding the one who commits bestiality: "He is stoned, and she [the animal] is stoned with the stones he was stoned with, and his trace is wiped out."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا حَفْصٌ، عَنْ لَيْثٍ، عَنِ الْحَكَمِ، قَالَ: مَنْ أَتَى الْبَهِيمَةَ أُقِيمَ عَلَيْهِ الْحَدُّ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Hafs -> Layth -> Al-Hakam
english.text: Al-Hakam said: "Whoever commits bestiality, the Hadd punishment is carried out on him."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ فُضَيْلٍ، عَنِ الْعَلَاءِ بْنِ الْمُسَيِّبِ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، قَالَ: مَنْ أَتَى بَهِيمَةً لَمْ تَقُمْ لَهُ قِيَامَةٌ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Ibn Fudayl -> Al-'Ala' ibn al-Musayyib -> His father
english.text: Al-Musayyib said: "Whoever commits bestiality will have no standing [on the Day of Judgment]."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْأَعْلَى، عَنْ مَعْمَرٍ، عَنِ الزُّهْرِيِّ، فِي الَّذِي يَأْتِي الْبَهِيمَةَ، قَالَ: عَلَيْهِ أَدْنَى الْحَدَّيْنِ أُحْصِنَ أَمْ لَمْ يُحْصَنْ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> 'Abd al-A'la -> Ma'mar -> Al-Zuhri
english.text: Al-Zuhri said regarding the one who commits bestiality: "Upon him is the lesser of the two Hadd punishments, whether he is married (Muhsan) or unmarried."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عُبَيْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ مُوسَى، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ بْنِ إِسْمَاعِيلَ، عَنْ دَاوُدَ بْنِ حُصَيْنٍ، عَنْ عِكْرِمَةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ عَنِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ، قَالَ: اقْتُلُوا الْفَاعِلَ بِالْبَهِيمَةِ وَالْبَهِيمَةَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> 'Ubayd Allah ibn Musa -> Ibrahim ibn Isma'il -> Dawud ibn Husayn -> 'Ikrimah -> Ibn 'Abbas
english.text: Ibn Abbas reported from the Prophet (saw): "Kill the one who commits bestiality and the animal."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ إِسْمَاعِيلَ بْنِ أَبِي خَالِدٍ، عَنْ عُمَيْرِ بْنِ نُمَيْرٍ، قَالَ: سُئِلَ ابْنُ عُمَرَ عَنْ جَارِيَةٍ كَانَتْ بَيْنَ رَجُلَيْنِ، فَوَقَعَ عَلَيْهَا أَحَدُهُمَا، قَالَ: لَيْسَ عَلَيْهَا حَدٌّ، هُوَ خَائِنٌ يُقَوَّمُ عَلَيْهِ قِيمَتُهَا وَيَأْخُذُهَا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Waki' -> Isma'il ibn Abi Khalid -> 'Umayr ibn Numayr
english.text: Ibn Umar was asked about a slave girl shared between two men, and one of them had intercourse with her. He said: "There is no Hadd punishment on her. He is treacherous; her value is estimated against him, and he takes her."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا حَفْصٌ، عَنْ دَاوُدَ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ الْمُسَيِّبِ، فِي جَارِيَةٍ كَانَتْ بَيْنَ رَجُلَيْنِ فَوَقَعَ عَلَيْهَا أَحَدُهُمَا، قَالَ: يُضْرَبُ تِسْعَةً وَتِسْعِينَ سَوْطًا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Hafs -> Dawud -> Sa'id ibn al-Musayyib
english.text: Sa'id ibn al-Musayyib said regarding a slave girl shared between two men, and one of them had intercourse with her: "He is flogged ninety-nine lashes."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا حَفْصٌ، عَنْ حَجَّاجٍ، عَنْ عَبْدَةَ، عَنْ شُرَيْحٍ أَنَّهُ: دَرَأَ عَنْهُ الْحَدَّ وَضَمَّنَهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Hafs -> Hajjaj -> 'Abdah -> Shurayh
english.text: Shurayh warded off the Hadd punishment from him and made him liable [for compensation].

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الرَّزَّاقِ، عَنْ مَعْمَرٍ، عَنِ الزُّهْرِيِّ، فِي الْأَمَةِ تَكُونُ بَيْنَ الشُّرَكَاءِ فَيَقَعُ عَلَيْهَا أَحَدُهُمْ، قَالَ: يُضْرَبُ مِائَةً

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> 'Abd al-Razzaq -> Ma'mar -> Al-Zuhri
english.text: Al-Zuhri said regarding a slave girl shared among partners, and one of them has intercourse with her: "He is flogged one hundred lashes."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا رَوَّادُ بْنُ الْجَرَّاحِ، عَنِ الْأَوْزَاعِيِّ، عَنْ مَكْحُولٍ، فِي جَارِيَةٍ بَيْنَ ثَلَاثَةٍ وَقَعَ عَلَيْهَا أَحَدُهُمْ، قَالَ: عَلَيْهِ أَدْنَى الْحَدَّيْنِ مِائَةٌ، وَعَلَيْهِ ثُلُثَا ثَمَنِهَا وَعُقْرُهَا وَثُلُثَا قِيمَةِ الْوَلَدِ إِنْ كَانَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Rawwad ibn al-Jarrah -> Al-Awza'i -> Makhul
english.text: Makhul said regarding a slave girl shared among three men, and one of them had intercourse with her: "Upon him is the lesser of the two Hadd punishments, one hundred lashes. And he owes two-thirds of her price, her dowry (Uqr), and two-thirds of the value of the child if there is one."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا يَزِيدُ، عَنْ هِشَامٍ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، قَالَ: يُعَزَّرُ وَيُقَوَّمُ عَلَيْهِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Yazid -> Hisham -> Al-Hasan
english.text: Al-Hasan said: "He is disciplined (Ta'zir) and her value is estimated against him."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا كَثِيرُ بْنُ هِشَامٍ، عَنْ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ بُرْقَانَ، قَالَ: بَلَغَنَا أَنَّ عُمَرَ بْنَ عَبْدِ الْعَزِيزِ، أُتِيَ بِجَارِيَةٍ كَانَتْ بَيْنَ رَجُلَيْنِ، فَوَطِئَهَا أَحَدُهُمَا، وَاسْتَشَارَ فِيهَا سَعِيدَ بْنَ الْمُسَيِّبِ، وعُرْوَةَ بْنِ الزُّبَيْرِ، فَقَالَا: نَرَى أَنْ يُجْلَدَ دُونَ الْحَدِّ وَيُقَيِّمُونَهَا قِيمَةً فَيَدْفَعُ إِلَى شَرِيكِهِ نِصْفَ الْقِيمَةِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Kathir ibn Hisham -> Ja'far ibn Burqan
english.text: Ja'far ibn Burqan reported: We were informed that Umar ibn Abd al-Aziz was brought a case of a slave girl shared between two men, one of whom had intercourse with her. He consulted Sa'id ibn al-Musayyib and Urwah ibn al-Zubayr regarding it. They said: "We see that he should be flogged less than the Hadd punishment, and they should value her, and he pays half the value to his partner."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْأَعْلَى، عَنْ يُونُسَ، فِي رَجُلٍ يَقَعُ عَلَى جَارِيَةٍ بَيْنَهُ وَبَيْنَ شَرِيكِهِ، قَالَ: تُقَوَّمُ عَلَيْهِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> 'Abd al-A'la -> Yunus
english.text: Yunus said regarding a man who has intercourse with a slave girl shared between him and his partner: "She is valued against him."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا يَحْيَى بْنُ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، فِي جَارِيَةٍ كَانَتْ بَيْنَ رَجُلَيْنِ فَوَقَعَ عَلَيْهَا أَحَدُهُمَا فَحَمَلَتْ، قَالَ: تُقَوَّمُ عَلَيْهِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Yahya ibn Sa'id -> Mughirah -> Ibrahim
english.text: Ibrahim said regarding a slave girl shared between two men, and one of them had intercourse with her and she became pregnant: "She is valued against him."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا حُمَيْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ، عَنْ حَسَنٍ، عَنْ لَيْثٍ، عَنْ طَاوُسٍ، فِي الْجَارِيَةِ تَكُونُ بَيْنَ الرَّجُلَيْنِ فَيَطَؤُهَا أَحَدُهُمَا، قَالَ: عَلَيْهِ الْعُقْرُ بِالْحِصَّةِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Humayd ibn 'Abd al-Rahman -> Hasan -> Layth -> Tawus
english.text: Tawus said regarding a slave girl shared between two men, and one of them has intercourse with her: "He owes the dowry (Uqr) according to the share."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، عَنْ إِسْمَاعِيلَ بْنِ سَالِمٍ، عَنِ الْحَكَمِ، أَنَّهُ، قَالَ فِي رَجُلٍ وَطِئَ جَارِيَةً مِنَ الْفَيْءِ، قَالَ: لَيْسَ عَلَيْهِ حَدٌّ، لَهُ فِيهَا نَصِيبٌ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Hushaym -> Isma'il ibn Salim -> Al-Hakam
english.text: Al-Hakam said regarding a man who had intercourse with a slave girl from the booty (Fay'): "There is no Hadd punishment on him; he has a share in her."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدَةُ، عَنْ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ الْمُسَيِّبِ، قَالَ: لَيْسَ عَلَيْهِ حَدٌّ إِذَا كَانَ لَهُ فِيهَا نَصِيبٌ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> 'Abdah -> Sa'id -> Qatadah -> Sa'id ibn al-Musayyib
english.text: Sa'id ibn al-Musayyib said: "There is no Hadd punishment on him if he has a share in her."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ مُوسَى بْنِ عُبَيْدَةَ، عَنْ بَكْرِ بْنِ دَاوُدَ: أَنَّ عَلِيًّا أَقَامَ عَلَى رَجُلٍ وَقَعَ عَلَى جَارِيَةٍ مِنَ الْخُمُسِ الْحَدَّ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Waki' -> Musa ibn 'Ubaydah -> Bakr ibn Dawud
english.text: Bakr ibn Dawud reported that Ali carried out the Hadd punishment on a man who had intercourse with a slave girl from the Khums (one-fifth share of booty).

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا يَزِيدُ بْنُ هَارُونَ، عَنْ هِشَامٍ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، قَالَ: إِذَا كَانَ لَهُ فِي الْفَيْءِ شَيْءٌ عُذِرَ، وَيُقَوَّمُ عَلَيْهِ، وَكَذَلِكَ فِي جَارِيَةٍ بَيْنَهُ وَبَيْنَ رَجُلَيْنِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Yazid ibn Harun -> Hisham -> Al-Hasan
english.text: Al-Hasan said: "If he has a share in the Fay', he is excused [from Hadd] and her value is estimated against him. The same applies to a slave girl shared between him and two other men."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، عَنْ أَبِي بِشْرٍ، عَنْ حَبِيبِ بْنِ سَالِمٍ: أَنَّ رَجُلًا وَقَعَ بِجَارِيَةِ امْرَأَتِهِ، فَأَتَتِ امْرَأَتُهُ النُّعْمَانَ بْنَ بَشِيرٍ فَأَخْبَرَتْهُ، قَالَ: أَمَا إِنَّ عِنْدِي فِي ذَلِكَ خَبَرًا شَافِيًا عَنْ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ، إِنْ كُنْتَ أَذِنْتَ لَهُ جَلَدْتُهُ مِائَةً، وَإِنْ كُنْتَ لَمْ تَأْذَنِي لَهُ رَجَمْتُهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Hushaym -> Abu Bishr -> Habib ibn Salim
english.text: Habib ibn Salim reported that a man had intercourse with his wife's slave girl. His wife came to Al-Nu'man ibn Bashir and informed him. He said: "I have a decisive report from the Messenger of Allah (saw) regarding this: If you permitted him, I will flog him one hundred lashes. If you did not permit him, I will stone him."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ مُسْهِرٍ، عَنِ الشَّيْبَانِيِّ، عَنْ عِكْرِمَةَ، قَالَ: جَاءَتِ امْرَأَةٌ إِلَى عَلِيٍّ، فَقَالَتْ: إِنَّ زَوْجِي وَقَعَ عَلَى وَلِيدَتِي، فَقَالَ: إِنْ تَكُونِي صَادِقَةً رَجَمْنَاهُ، وَإِنْ تَكُونِي كَاذِبَةً جَلَدْنَاكَ ثُمَّ تَصَبَّرَتِ النَّاسَ حَتَّى اخْتَلَطُوا، فَذَهَبَتِ الْمَرْأَةُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Ibn Mushir -> Al-Shaybani -> 'Ikrimah
english.text: Ikrimah said: A woman came to Ali and said: "My husband had intercourse with my slave girl." He said: "If you are telling the truth, we will stone him. If you are lying, we will flog you." Then the people waited patiently until they mixed, and the woman left.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ إِسْمَاعِيلَ بْنِ أَبِي خَالِدٍ، عَنْ مُبَارَكِ بْنِ عُمَارَةَ، قَالَ: جَاءَتِ امْرَأَةٌ إِلَى عَلِيٍّ، فَقَالَتْ: يَا وَيْلَهَا إِنَّ زَوْجَهَا وَقَعَ عَلَى جَارِيَتِهَا، فَقَالَ: إِنْ كُنْتِ صَادِقَةً رَجَمْنَاهُ، وَإِنْ كُنْتِ كَاذِبَةً جَلَدْنَاكَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Waki' -> Isma'il ibn Abi Khalid -> Mubarak ibn 'Umarah
english.text: Mubarak ibn 'Umarah reported that a woman came to Ali saying: "Woe to her! Her husband had intercourse with her slave girl." He said: "If you are truthful, we will stone him. If you are lying, we will flog you."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو مُعَاوِيَةَ، عَنْ حَجَّاجٍ، عَنْ مَكْحُولٍ، قَالَ: قَالَ عُمَرُ: لَا أُوتَى بِرَجُلٍ وَقَعَ عَلَى جَارِيَةِ امْرَأَتِهِ إِلَّا فَعَلْتُ وَفَعَلْتُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Abu Mu'awiyah -> Hajjaj -> Makhul
english.text: Umar said: "I will not be brought a man who had intercourse with his wife's slave girl except that I will do such and such [punish him severely]."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ إِدْرِيسَ، عَنْ هِشَامٍ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، وَابْنِ سِيرِينَ كَانَا إِذَا سُئِلَا عَنِ الرَّجُلِ يَقَعُ عَلَى جَارِيَةِ امْرَأَتِهِ يَتْلُوَانِ هَذِهِ الْآيَةَ: " {وَالَّذِينَ هُمْ لِفُرُوجِهِمْ حَافِظُونَ إِلَّا عَلَى أَزْوَاجِهِمْ أَوْ مَا مَلَكَتْ أَيْمَانُهُمْ فَإِنَّهُمْ غَيْرُ مَلُومِينَ} [المؤمنون: 6]

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Ibn Idris -> Hisham -> Al-Hasan and Ibn Sirin
english.text: Al-Hasan and Ibn Sirin, when asked about a man having intercourse with his wife's slave girl, would recite this verse: "And they who guard their private parts. Except from their wives or those their right hands possess, for indeed, they will not be blamed" (Al-Mu'minun: 5-6).

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ بِشْرِ بْنِ سُلَيْمَانَ، قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، يَقُولُ: يُعَزَّرُ وَلَا حَدَّ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Waki' -> Bishr ibn Sulayman -> Ibrahim
english.text: Ibrahim said: "He is disciplined (Ta'zir) but there is no Hadd punishment."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ إِسْرَائِيلَ، عَنْ سِمَاكٍ، عَنْ مَعْبَدٍ، وعُبَيْدٍ، ابْنَيْ حُمْرَانَ، عَنِ ابْنِ مَسْعُودٍ: أَنَّهُ ضَرَبَهُ دُونَ الْحَدِّ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Waki' -> Isra'il -> Simak -> Ma'bad and 'Ubayd, sons of Humran -> Ibn Mas'ud
english.text: Ibn Mas'ud flogged him less than the Hadd punishment.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنِ الْأَعْمَشِ، قَالَ: قَالَ عَلْقَمَةُ: "مَا أُبَالِي وَقَعْتُ عَلَى جَارِيَةِ امْرَأَتِي أَوْ جَارِيَةِ عَوْسَجَةَ: رَجُلٍ مِنَ الْحَيِّ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Waki' -> Al-A'mash -> 'Alqamah
english.text: Alqamah said: "It makes no difference to me whether I have intercourse with my wife's slave girl or the slave girl of 'Awsajah, a man from the tribe [it is equally forbidden]."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا غُنْدَرٌ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، عَنْ أَبِي إِسْحَاقَ، عَنْ أَبِي مَيْسَرَةَ، فِي رَجُلٍ يَأْتِي جَارِيَةَ امْرَأَتِهِ أَنَّهُ، قَالَ: مَا أُبَالِي أَتَيْتُهَا أَوْ جَارِيَةً مِنَ الطَّرِيقِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Ghundar -> Shu'bah -> Abu Ishaq -> Abu Maysarah
english.text: Abu Maysarah said regarding a man who has intercourse with his wife's slave girl: "It makes no difference to me whether I have intercourse with her or a slave girl from the road."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْأَعْلَى، عَنْ هِشَامٍ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، قَالَ: عَلَيْهِ الْحَدُّ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> 'Abd al-A'la -> Hisham -> Al-Hasan
english.text: Al-Hasan said: "The Hadd punishment is upon him."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ عَامِرٍ، عَنْ سَالِمٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ، قَالَ: قَالَ عُمَرُ: لَوْ أُتِيتُ بِرَجُلٍ وَقَعَ عَلَى جَارِيَةِ امْرَأَتِهِ لَرَجَمْتُهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Waki' -> Sufyan -> 'Amir -> Salim -> Ibn 'Umar
english.text: Umar said: "If a man who had intercourse with his wife's slave girl were brought to me, I would stone him."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا يَزِيدُ بْنُ هَارُونَ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ أَبِي عَرُوبَةَ، عَنْ إِيَاسِ بْنِ مُعَاوِيَةَ، عَنْ نَافِعٍ، قَالَ: جَاءَتْ جَارِيَةٌ إِلَى عُمَرَ، فَقَالَتْ: يَا أَمِيرَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ، إِنَّ الْمُغِيرَةَ يَطَؤُنِي، وَإِنَّ امْرَأَتَهُ تَدْعُونِي زَانِيَةً، فَإِنْ كُنْتُ لَهَا فَانْهَهُ عَنْ غَشَيَانِي، وَإِنْ كُنْتُ لَهُ فَانْهَ امْرَأَتَهُ عَنْ قَذْفِي، فَأَرْسَلَ إِلَى الْمُغِيرَةِ، فَقَالَ: تَطَأُ هَذِهِ الْجَارِيَةَ؟، قَالَ: نَعَمْ، قَالَ: مِنْ أَيْنَ؟، قَالَ: وَهَبَتْهَا لِي امْرَأَتِي، قَالَ: وَاللَّهِ لَئِنْ لَمْ تَكُنْ وَهَبَتْهَا لَكَ لَتَرْجِعُ إِلَى أَهْلِكَ مَرْجُومًا ثُمَّ......، وَقَالَ: انْطَلِقَا إِلَى امْرَأَةِ الْمُغِيرَةِ فَأَعْلِمَاهَا لَئِنْ لَمْ تَكُونِي وَهَبْتِهَا لَنَرْجُمَنَّهُ، قَالَ: فَأَتَيَاهَا فَأَخْبِرَاهَا، فَقَالَتْ: يَا لَهْفَاهُ، أَتُرِيدُ أَنْ يُرْجَمَ بَعْلِي لَاهَا اللَّهِ إِذًا لَقَدْ وَهَبْتُهَا لَهُ، قَالَ: فَخَلَّى عَنْهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Yazid ibn Harun -> Ibn Abi 'Arubah -> Iyas ibn Mu'awiyah -> Nafi'
english.text: Nafi' reported that a slave girl came to Umar and said: "O Commander of the Faithful, Al-Mughirah is having intercourse with me, and his wife calls me an adulteress. If I belong to her, forbid him from being intimate with me; if I belong to him, forbid his wife from slandering me." He sent for Al-Mughirah and said: "Do you have intercourse with this slave girl?" He said: "Yes." He said: "How?" He said: "My wife gifted her to me." He said: "By Allah, if she did not gift her to you, you will return to your family stoned." ... He said: "Go to Al-Mughirah's wife and tell her: 'If you did not gift her, we will stone him.'" They went to her and informed her. She said: "Woe is me! Do you want my husband to be stoned? By Allah, I have gifted her to him." So he let him go.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ فُضَيْلٍ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، قَالَ: أَتَى رَجُلٌ ابْنَ مَسْعُودٍ، فَقَالَ: إِنِّي وَقَعْتُ عَلَى جَارِيَةِ امْرَأَتِي، فَقَالَ: قَدْ سَتَرَ اللَّهُ عَلَيْكَ فَاسْتُرْ، فَبَلَغَ ذَلِكَ عَلِيًّا، فَقَالَ: لَوْ أَتَانِي الَّذِي أَتَى ابْنَ أُمِّ عَبْدٍ لَرَضَخْتُ رَأْسَهُ بِالْحِجَارَةِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Ibn Fudayl -> Mughirah
english.text: Mughirah reported that a man came to Ibn Mas'ud and said: "I had intercourse with my wife's slave girl." He said: "Allah has concealed you, so conceal yourself." This reached Ali, who said: "If the one who came to Ibn Umm Abd had come to me, I would have smashed his head with stones."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، عَنِ الْهَيْثَمِ بْنِ بَدْرٍ، عَنْ حُرْقُوصٍ، عَنْ عَلِيٍّ: أَنَّ رَجُلًا وَقَعَ عَلَى جَارِيَةِ امْرَأَتِهِ، فَدَرَأَ عَنْهُ الْحَدَّ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Waki' -> Sufyan -> Mughirah -> Al-Haytham ibn Badr -> Hurqus -> Ali
english.text: Ali warded off the Hadd punishment from a man who had intercourse with his wife's slave girl.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ إِسْمَاعِيلَ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ، أَنَّهُ جَاءَ إِلَيْهِ رَجُلٌ، فَقَالَ: إِنِّي وَقَعْتُ عَلَى جَارِيَةِ امْرَأَتِي، فَقَالَ: اتَّقِ اللَّهَ، وَلَا تَعُدْ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Waki' -> Isma'il -> Al-Sha'bi -> 'Abd Allah
english.text: A man came to Abdullah and said: "I had intercourse with my wife's slave girl." He said: "Fear Allah and do not do it again."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ بِشْرٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ مَنْصُورٍ، عَنْ رِبْعِيٍّ، عَنْ عُقْبَةَ بْنِ حَيَّانَ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ، قَالَ: لَا حَدَّ عَلَيْهِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Muhammad ibn Bishr -> Sufyan -> Mansur -> Rib'i -> 'Uqbah ibn Hayyan -> 'Abd Allah
english.text: Abdullah said: "There is no Hadd punishment upon him."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا يَحْيَى بْنُ سَعِيدٍ الْقَطَّانُ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنِ الشَّيْبَانِيِّ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ، عَنْ عَامِرِ بْنِ مَطَرٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ، فِي الرَّجُلِ يَقَعُ عَلَى جَارِيَةِ امْرَأَتِهِ، قَالَ: إِنْ اسْتَكْرَهَهَا فَهِيَ حُرَّةٌ، وَعَلَيْهِ مِثْلُهَا لِسَيِّدَتِهَا، وَإِنْ كَانَتْ طَاوَعَتْهُ فَهِيَ لَهُ وَعَلَيْهِ مِثْلُهَا لِسَيِّدَتِهَا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Yahya ibn Sa'id al-Qattan -> Sufyan -> Al-Shaybani -> Al-Sha'bi -> 'Amir ibn Matar -> 'Abd Allah
english.text: Abdullah said regarding a man who has intercourse with his wife's slave girl: "If he forced her, she is free, and he owes a similar one to her mistress. If she consented, she becomes his, and he owes a similar one to her mistress."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو دَاوُدَ الطَّيَالِسِيُّ، عَنْ جَرِيرٍ، عَنْ قَيْسٍ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، قَالَ: لَا حَدَّ عَلَيْهِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Abu Dawud al-Tayalisi -> Jarir -> Qays -> 'Ata'
english.text: Ata said: "There is no Hadd punishment upon him."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ السَّلَامِ، عَنْ هِشَامٍ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، عَنْ سَلَمَةَ بْنِ مُحَبِّقٍ: أَنَّ رَجُلًا وَقَعَ عَلَى جَارِيَةِ امْرَأَتِهِ، فَدَرَأَ عَنْهُ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ الْحَدَّ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> 'Abd al-Salam -> Hisham -> Al-Hasan -> Salamah ibn Muhabbiq
english.text: Salamah ibn Muhabbiq reported that a man had intercourse with his wife's slave girl, and the Prophet (saw) warded off the Hadd punishment from him.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو مُحَمَّدٍ عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ يُونُسَ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بَقِيُّ بْنُ مَخْلَدٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ هِشَامٍ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ الْمُسَيِّبِ: أَنَّ امْرَأَةً تَزَوَّجَتْ فِي عِدَّتِهَا فَضَرَبَهَا عُمَرُ تَعْزِيرًا دُونَ الْحَدِّ

english.narrator: Abu Muhammad 'Abd Allah ibn Yunus -> Abu 'Abd al-Rahman Baqi ibn Makhlad -> Abu Bakr -> Waki' -> Hisham -> Qatadah -> Sa'id ibn al-Musayyib
english.text: Sa'id ibn al-Musayyib reported that a woman married during her waiting period (Iddah), and Umar flogged her as discipline (Ta'zir) less than the Hadd.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْأَعْلَى، عَنْ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، قَالَ: قُلْتُ لِسَعِيدِ بْنِ الْمُسَيِّبِ: إِنْ تَزَوَّجَهَا فِي عِدَّتِهَا عَمْدًا؟، قَالَ: يُقَامُ عَلَيْهَا الْحَدُّ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> 'Abd al-A'la -> Sa'id -> Qatadah
english.text: Qatadah said: I asked Sa'id ibn al-Musayyib: "If he marries her intentionally during her Iddah?" He said: "The Hadd punishment is carried out on her."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْأَعْلَى، عَنْ مَعْمَرٍ، عَنِ الزُّهْرِيِّ: "أَنَّ مَرْوَانَ جَلَدَهُمَا أَرْبَعِينَ أَرْبَعِينَ، وَفَرَّقَ بَيْنَهُمَا، فَقَالَ لَهُ قَبِيصَةُ بْنُ ذُؤَيْبٍ: لَوْ خَفَّفْتُ فَجَلَدْتُهُمَا عِشْرِينَ عِشْرِينَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> 'Abd al-A'la -> Ma'mar -> Al-Zuhri
english.text: Al-Zuhri reported that Marwan flogged them forty lashes each and separated them. Qabisah ibn Dhu'ayb said to him: "If only you had lightened it and flogged them twenty each."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ إِسْرَائِيلَ، عَنْ جَابِرٍ، عَنْ عَامِرٍ، وعَنْ حَمَّادٍ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، فِي امْرَأَةٍ نَكَحَتْ فِي عِدَّتِهَا، قَالَا: لَيْسَ عَلَيْهَا حَدٌّ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Waki' -> Isra'il -> Jabir -> 'Amir -> Hammad -> Ibrahim
english.text: 'Amir and Ibrahim said regarding a woman who married during her Iddah: "There is no Hadd punishment upon her."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا شَرِيكٌ، عَنْ مَنْصُورٍ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: لَا يُقَامُ عَلَى أَهْلِ الْكِتَابِ حَدٌّ فِي شُرْبِ خَمْرٍ، وَلَا زِنًا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Sharik -> Mansur -> Ibrahim
english.text: Ibrahim said: "No Hadd punishment is carried out on the People of the Book for drinking wine or adultery."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، عَنْ عَمْرٍو، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، قَالَ: لَيْسَ عَلَى أَهْلِ الْكِتَابِ حَدٌّ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Waki' -> Shu'bah -> 'Amr -> Mujahid -> Ibn 'Abbas
english.text: Ibn Abbas said: "There is no Hadd punishment on the People of the Book."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عِيسَى بْنُ يُونُسَ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي عَرُوبَةَ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، عَنْ رَجَاءٍ، عَنْ قَبِيصَةَ بْنِ ذُؤَيْبٍ: أَنَّ رَجُلًا وَقَعَ عَلَى جَارِيَتِهِ وَلَهَا زَوْجٌ، فَضَرَبَهُ عُمَرُ بْنُ الْخَطَّابِ مِائَةً نَكَالًا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> 'Isa ibn Yunus -> Ibn Abi 'Arubah -> Qatadah -> Raja' -> Qabisah ibn Dhu'ayb
english.text: Qabisah ibn Dhu'ayb reported that a man had intercourse with his slave girl who was married. Umar ibn al-Khattab flogged him one hundred lashes as an exemplary punishment.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا شَرِيكٌ، عَنْ جَامِعٍ، عَنْ زَيْدِ بْنِ أَسْلَمَ، قَالَ: أُوتِيَ عُمَرُ بِرَجُلٍ وَقَعَ عَلَى أَمَتِهِ وَقَدْ زَوَّجَهَا، فَضَرَبَهُ ضَرْبًا وَلَمْ يَبْلُغْ بِهِ الْحَدَّ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Sharik -> Jami' -> Zayd ibn Aslam
english.text: Zayd ibn Aslam said: "Umar was brought a man who had intercourse with his slave girl whom he had married off. He beat him severely but did not reach the extent of the Hadd punishment."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْأَعْلَى، عَنْ مَعْمَرٍ، عَنِ الزُّهْرِيِّ، قَالَ: إِذَا وَقَعَ الرَّجُلُ عَلَى أَمَتِهِ وَلَهَا زَوْجٌ، فَإِنَّهُ يُجْلَدُ مِائَةً أُحْصِنَ أَمْ لَمْ يُحْصَنْ، فَإِنْ حَمَلَتْ فَالْوَلَدُ لِلْفِرَاشِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> 'Abd al-A'la -> Ma'mar -> Al-Zuhri
english.text: Al-Zuhri said: "If a man has intercourse with his slave girl who has a husband, he is flogged one hundred lashes, whether he is married or not. If she becomes pregnant, the child belongs to the bed (husband)."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا غُنْدَرٌ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ حَمَّادًا عَنِ الرَّجُلِ يَسْرِقُ مِنْ بَيْتِ الْمَالِ، قَالَ: يُقْطَعُ وَقَالَ الْحَكَمُ: لَا يُقْطَعُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Ghundar -> Shu'bah
english.text: Shu'bah asked Hammad about a man who steals from the public treasury (Bayt al-Mal). He said: "He is cut." Al-Hakam said: "He is not cut."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنِ الْمَسْعُودِيِّ، عَنِ الْقَاسِمِ: أَنَّ رَجُلًا سَرَقَ مِنْ بَيْتِ الْمَالِ، فَكَتَبَ فِيهِ سَعْدٌ إِلَى عُمَرَ، فَكَتَبَ عُمَرُ إِلَى سَعْدٍ: لَيْسَ عَلَيْهِ قَطْعٌ، لَهُ فِيهِ نَصِيبٌ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Waki' -> Al-Mas'udi -> Al-Qasim
english.text: Al-Qasim reported that a man stole from the public treasury. Sa'd wrote to Umar about him, and Umar wrote back to Sa'd: "There is no cutting for him; he has a share in it."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ الْحَكَمَ يَسْرِقُ مِنْ بَيْتِ الْمَالِ، قَالَ: لَيْسَ عَلَيْهِ قَطْعٌ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Waki' -> Shu'bah
english.text: Shu'bah asked Al-Hakam about stealing from the public treasury. He said: "There is no cutting for him."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدَةُ بْنُ سُلَيْمَانَ، عَنْ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ الْمُسَيِّبِ فِي الرَّجُلِ يَسْرِقُ مِنَ الْمَغْنَمِ، قَالَ: لَيْسَ عَلَيْهِ حَدٌّ إِذَا كَانَ لَهُ فِيهِ نَصِيبٌ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> 'Abdah ibn Sulayman -> Sa'id -> Qatadah -> Sa'id ibn al-Musayyib
english.text: Sa'id ibn al-Musayyib said regarding a man who steals from the spoils of war: "There is no Hadd punishment upon him if he has a share in it."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا يَزِيدُ بْنُ هَارُونَ، عَنْ هِشَامٍ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، قَالَ: إِذَا سَرَقَ الرَّجُلُ مِنَ الْغَنِيمَةِ وَلَهُ فِيهَا شَيْءٌ لَمْ يُقْطَعْ، فَإِنْ سَرَقَ مِنْهَا وَلَيْسَ لَهُ فِيهَا نَصِيبٌ قُطِعَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Yazid ibn Harun -> Hisham -> Al-Hasan
english.text: Al-Hasan said: "If a man steals from the booty and has a share in it, he is not cut. If he steals from it and has no share in it, he is cut."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا شَرِيكٌ، عَنْ سِمَاكٍ، عَنْ أَبِي عُبَيْدِ بْنِ الْأَبْرَصِ: أَنَّ عَلِيًّا كَانَ يَقْسِمُ سِلَاحًا فِي الرَّحْبَةِ، فَأَخَذَ رَجُلٌ مِغْفَرًا فَالْتَحَفَ عَلَيْهِ، فَوَجَدَهُ رَجُلٌ، فَأَتَى بِهِ عَلِيًّا فَلَمْ يَقْطَعْهُ، وَقَالَ: لَهُ فِيهِ شِرْكٌ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Sharik -> Simak -> Abu 'Ubayd ibn al-Abras
english.text: Abu 'Ubayd ibn al-Abrras reported that Ali was distributing weapons in the courtyard. A man took a helmet and hid it under his wrap. Another man found him and brought him to Ali. Ali did not cut him and said: "He has a share in it."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ عُيَيْنَةَ، عَنِ الزُّهْرِيِّ، عَنِ السَّائِبِ بْنِ يَزِيدَ: أَنَّ عَبْدَ اللَّهِ بْنَ عَمْرِو بْنِ الْحَضْرَمِيِّ، قَالَ: أَتَيْتُ عُمَرَ بِغُلَامٍ لِي فَقُلْتُ: اقْطَعْهُ، قَالَ: وَمَا لَهُ؟ قُلْتُ: سَرَقَ مِرْآةً لِامْرَأَتِي خَيْرٌ مِنْ سِتِّينَ دِرْهَمًا، قَالَ عُمَرُ: غُلَامُكُمْ سَرَقَ مَتَاعَكُمْ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Ibn 'Uyaynah -> Al-Zuhri -> Al-Sa'ib ibn Yazid
english.text: Abdullah ibn 'Amr ibn al-Hadrami said: I brought a slave of mine to Umar and said: "Cut him." He asked: "What did he do?" I said: "He stole a mirror belonging to my wife worth more than sixty dirhams." Umar said: "Your slave stole your goods."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو مُعَاوِيَةَ، عَنِ الْأَعْمَشِ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، عَنْ هَمَّامٍ، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ شُرَحْبِيلَ، قَالَ: جَاءَ مَعْقِلٌ الْمُزَنِي إِلَى عَبْدِ اللَّهِ، فَقَالَ: غُلَامِي سَرَقَ قَبَائِي فَأَقْطَعُهُ؟، قَالَ عَبْدُ اللَّهِ: لَا , مالُكَ بَعْضُهُ مِنْ بَعْضٍ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Abu Mu'awiyah -> Al-A'mash -> Ibrahim -> Hammam -> 'Amr ibn Shurahbil
english.text: Ma'qil al-Muzani came to Abdullah and said: "My slave stole my cloak. Should I cut him?" Abdullah said: "No, your wealth is part of one another."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا يَزِيدُ بْنُ هَارُونَ، عَنْ حَجَّاجٍ، عَنِ الْحَكَمِ: أَنَّ عَلِيًّا، قَالَ: إِذَا سَرَقَ عَبْدِي مِنْ مَالِي لَمْ أَقْطَعْهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Yazid ibn Harun -> Hajjaj -> Al-Hakam
english.text: Ali said: "If my slave steals from my wealth, I do not cut him."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا سُلَيْمُ بْنُ حَيَّانَ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا سَعِيدُ بْنُ مِينَاءَ، قَالَ: كَانَ عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ الزُّبَيْرِ يَلِي صَدَقَةَ الزُّبَيْرِ، وَكَانَتْ فِي بَيْتٍ لَا يَدْخُلُهُ أَحَدٌ غَيْرُهُ وَغَيْرُ جَارِيَةٍ لَهُ، فَفَقَدَ شَيْئًا مِنَ الْمَالِ، فَقَالَ لِلْجَارِيَةِ: مَا كَانَ يَدْخُلُ هَذَا الْبَيْتَ غَيْرِي وَغَيْرُكَ، فَمَنْ أَخَذَ هَذَا الْمَالَ؟ فَأَقَرَّتِ الْجَارِيَةُ، فَقَالَ لِي: يَا سَعِيدُ انْطَلِقْ بِهَا فَاقْطَعْ يَدَهَا، فَإِنَّ الْمَالَ لَوْ كَانَ لِي لَمْ يَكُنْ عَلَيْهَا قَطْعٌ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Sulaym ibn Hayyan -> Sa'id ibn Mina
english.text: Sa'id ibn Mina said: Abdullah ibn al-Zubayr was in charge of the charity of Al-Zubayr, which was in a house that no one entered except him and a slave girl of his. Some money went missing. He said to the slave girl: "No one entered this house except me and you. Who took this money?" The slave girl confessed. He said to me: "O Sa'id, take her and cut off her hand. For if the money were mine, there would be no cutting for her."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا غُنْدَرٌ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ حَمَّادًا، وَالْحَكَمَ عَنِ الرَّجُلِ يَقَعُ عَلَى جَارِيَةِ أُمِّهِ، قَالَا: عَلَيْهِ الْحَدُّ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Ghundar -> Shu'bah
english.text: Shu'bah asked Hammad and Al-Hakam about a man who has intercourse with his mother's slave girl. They said: "The Hadd punishment is upon him."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا غُنْدَرٌ، عَنْ أَشْعَثَ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، قَالَ: لَيْسَ عَلَيْهِ حَدٌّ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Ghundar -> Ash'ath -> Al-Hasan
english.text: Al-Hasan said: "There is no Hadd punishment upon him."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ الْأَقْمَرِ، عَنْ يَزِيدَ بْنِ أَبِي كَبْشَةَ: أَنَّ أَبَا الدَّرْدَاءِ، أُتِيَ بِامْرَأَةٍ قَدْ سَرَقَتْ، فَقَالَ لَهَا: "سَلَامَةُ أَسَرَقْتِ؟ قُولِي: لَا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Waki' -> Sufyan -> Ali ibn al-Aqmar -> Yazid ibn Abi Kabshah
english.text: Yazid ibn Abi Kabshah reported that Abu al-Darda' was brought a woman named Salamah who had stolen. He said to her: "O Salamah, did you steal? Say: No."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا شَرِيكٌ، عَنْ جَابِرٍ، عَنْ مَوْلًى لِأَبِي مَسْعُودٍ، عَنْ أَبِي مَسْعُودٍ، قَالَ: أُتِيَ بِرَجُلٍ سَرَقَ، فَقَالَ: "أَسَرَقْتَ؟ قُلْ: وَجَدْتُهُ، قَالَ: وَجَدْتُهُ، فَخَلَّى سَبِيلَهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Sharik -> Jabir -> Mawla of Abu Mas'ud -> Abu Mas'ud
english.text: Abu Mas'ud was brought a man who had stolen. He said: "Did you steal? Say: 'I found it.'" The man said: "I found it." So he let him go.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ بِشْرٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا سَعِيدُ بْنُ أَبِي عَرُوبَةَ، عَنْ سُلَيْمَانَ النَّاجِي، عَنْ أَبِي الْمُتَوَكِّلِ: أَنَّ أَبَا هُرَيْرَةَ، أُتِيَ بِسَارِقٍ وَهُوَ يَوْمَئِذٍ أَمِيرٌ، فَقَالَ: "أَسَرَقْتَ؟ أَسَرَقْتَ؟ قُلْ: لَا قُلْ: لَا مَرَّتَيْنِ أَوْ ثَلَاثًا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Muhammad ibn Bishr -> Sa'id ibn Abi 'Arubah -> Sulayman al-Naji -> Abu al-Mutawakkil
english.text: Abu al-Mutawakkil reported that Abu Hurayrah, when he was governor, was brought a thief. He said: "Did you steal? Did you steal? Say: No. Say: No," twice or thrice.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ عُيَيْنَةَ، عَنْ يَزِيدَ بْنِ خُصَيْفَةَ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ ثَوْبَانَ: أَنَّ رَجُلًا سَرَقَ شَمْلَةً، فَأُتِيَ بِهِ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ، فَقَالُوا: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ. هَذَا سَرَقَ شَمْلَةً، فَقَالَ: مَا أَخَالُهُ سَرَقَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Ibn 'Uyaynah -> Yazid ibn Khusayfah -> Muhammad ibn 'Abd al-Rahman ibn Thawban
english.text: Muhammad ibn 'Abd al-Rahman ibn Thawban reported that a man stole a cloak and was brought to the Prophet (saw). They said: "O Messenger of Allah, this man stole a cloak." He said: "I do not think he stole."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا حُمَيْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ، عَنْ حَسَنِ بْنِ صَالِحٍ، عَنْ غَالِبٍ أَبِي الْهُذَيْلِ، قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ سُبَيْعًا أَبَا سَالِمٍ، يَقُولُ: شَهِدْتُ الْحَسَنَ بْنَ عَلِيٍّ وَأُتِيَ بِرَجُلٍ أَقَرَّ بِسَرِقَةٍ، فَقَالَ لَهُ الْحَسَنُ: "لَعَلَّكَ اخْتَلَسْتَ لِكَيْ يَقُولَ: لَا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Humayd ibn 'Abd al-Rahman -> Hasan ibn Salih -> Ghalib Abu al-Hudhayl -> Subay' Abu Salim
english.text: Subay' Abu Salim said: I witnessed Al-Hasan ibn Ali when a man was brought who confessed to theft. Al-Hasan said to him: "Perhaps you snatched it?" trying to make him say no.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ بَكْرٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ جُرَيْجٍ، عَنْ عِكْرِمَةَ بْنِ خَالِدٍ، قَالَ: أُتِيَ عُمَرُ بِسَارِقٍ قَدِ اعْتَرَفَ، فَقَالَ عُمَرُ: إِنِّي لَأَرَى يَدَ رَجُلٍ مَا هِيَ بِيَدِ سَارِقٍ، قَالَ الرَّجُلُ: وَاللَّهِ مَا أَنَا بِسَارِقٍ، فَأَرْسَلَهُ عُمَرُ وَلَمْ يَقْطَعْهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Muhammad ibn Bakr -> Ibn Jurayj -> 'Ikrimah ibn Khalid
english.text: Ikrimah ibn Khalid reported that Umar was brought a thief who had confessed. Umar said: "I see the hand of a man that is not the hand of a thief." The man said: "By Allah, I am not a thief." So Umar released him and did not cut his hand.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ بَكْرٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ جُرَيْجٍ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، يَقُولُ: "كَانَ مَنْ مَضَى يُؤْتَى بِالسَّارِقِ فَيَقُولُ: أَسَرَقْتَ؟ قُلْ: لَا وَلَا أَعْلَمُ إِلَّا سَمَّى أَبَا بَكْرٍ وَعُمَرَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Muhammad ibn Bakr -> Ibn Jurayj -> 'Ata'
english.text: Ata used to say: "Those who have passed used to be brought a thief, and they would say: 'Did you steal? Say: No.'" He said: I only know him to name Abu Bakr and Umar.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عِيسَى بْنُ يُونُسَ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَوْنٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنِي مِسْكِينٌ، رَجُلٌ مِنْ أَهْلِي، قَالَ: شَهِدْتَ عَلِيًّا أُتِيَ بِرَجُلٍ وَامْرَأَةٍ وُجِدَا فِي خَرِبَةٍ، فَقَالَ لَهُ عَلِيٌّ: أَقَرَبْتَهَا؟ فَجَعَلَ أَصْحَابُ عَلِيٍّ يَقُولُونَ لَهُ: قُلْ: لَا، فَقَالَ: لَا، فَخَلَّى سَبِيلَهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> 'Isa ibn Yunus -> Ibn 'Awn -> Miskin
english.text: Miskin said: I witnessed Ali being brought a man and a woman found in a ruin. Ali said to him: "Did you approach her?" Ali's companions started saying to him: "Say: No." He said: "No." So he released him.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ مُبَارَكٍ، عَنْ مَعْمَرٍ، عَنْ يَحْيَى، عَنْ عِكْرِمَةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، أَنَّ النَّبِيَّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ، قَالَ لِمَاعِزِ بْنِ مَالِكٍ: لَعَلَّكَ قَبَّلْتَ أَوْ لَمَسْتَ أَوْ بَاشَرْتَ؟

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Ibn Mubarak -> Ma'mar -> Yahya -> 'Ikrimah -> Ibn 'Abbas
english.text: Ibn Abbas reported that the Prophet (saw) said to Ma'iz ibn Malik: "Perhaps you kissed, or touched, or were intimate [without penetration]?"

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو خَالِدٍ، عَنْ يَحْيَى بْنِ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ يَحْيَى بْنِ حِبَّانَ، عَنْ رَافِعِ بْنِ خَدِيجٍ، قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: لَا قَطْعَ فِي ثَمَرٍ، وَلَا كَثَرٍ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr -> Abu Khalid -> Yahya ibn Sa'id -> Muhammad ibn Yahya ibn Hibban -> Rafi' ibn Khadij
english.text: Rafi' ibn Khadij reported that the Messenger of Allah (saw) said: "There is no cutting [of the hand] for fruit or palm pith."
